##     dhf_norm_6        0.994
##     dhf_norm_7        0.915
##     dhf_norm_8        0.976
##     dhf_norm_9        0.974
##     dhf_norm_10       0.940
##     dhf_norm_11       0.356
##     dhf_norm_12       0.657
##     dhf_norm_13       0.046
##     dhf_norm_14       0.906
##     dhf_norm_15       0.043
##     dhf_norm_16       0.887
##     dhf_norm_17       0.903
##     dhf_norm_18       0.990
##     dhf_norm_19       0.737
##     dhf_norm_20       0.067
##     dhf_norm_21       0.938
##     dhf_norm_22       0.819
##     dhf_norm_23       0.883
##     dhf_norm_24       0.590
##     dhf_norm_25       0.907
##     dhf_norm_26       0.816
##     dhf_norm_27       0.883
##     dhf_norm_28       0.279
##     dhf_norm_29       0.767
##     dhf_norm_30       0.876
##     D_self            0.651
##     H_self            0.589
##     F_self            0.969
##     F_other           0.970
hierarchical.individual.testc.fitindices <- fitmeasures(hierarchical.model.testc.fit)
hierarchical.model.testc.params <- tidy(hierarchical.model.testc.fit)

Endorsement 6-Factor Individual Test Combined

sixfactor.model.testc <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D_self =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5
  D_other =~ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
  H_self =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_13+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15
  H_other =~ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19+dhf_endors_20
  F_self =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25
  F_other =~ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30
  
Level: 2 
  D_self =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5
  D_other =~ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
  H_self =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_13+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15
  H_other =~ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19+dhf_endors_20
  F_self =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25
  F_other =~ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30

')

sixfactor.individual.testc.fit <- cfa(model = sixfactor.model.testc,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(sixfactor.individual.testc.fit, "output/sixfactor.individual.testc.fit")
sixfactor.individual.testc.fit <- readRDS("output/sixfactor.individual.testc.fit")

summary(sixfactor.individual.testc.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

##     dhf_endors_29     0.620
##     dhf_endors_30     0.725
sixfactor.individual.testc.fitindices <- fitmeasures(sixfactor.individual.testc.fit)
sixfactor.individual.testc.params <- tidy(sixfactor.individual.testc.fit)

Endorsement 3-Factor Individual Test Combined

threefactor.model.testc <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5+ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
   H =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_13+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15+ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19+dhf_endors_20
   F =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25+ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30


Level: 2 
   D =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5+ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
   H =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_13+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15+ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19+dhf_endors_20
   F =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25+ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30

')

threefactor.individual.testc.fit <- cfa(model = threefactor.model.testc,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(threefactor.individual.testc.fit, "output/threefactor.individual.testc.fit")
threefactor.individual.testc.fit <- readRDS("output/threefactor.individual.testc.fit")

summary(threefactor.individual.testc.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

##     dhf_endors_30     0.664
threefactor.individual.testc.fitindices <- fitmeasures(threefactor.individual.testc.fit)
threefactor.individual.testc.params <- tidy(threefactor.individual.testc.fit)

Endorsement 6+3-Factor Individual Test Combined

six3factor.model.testc <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D_self =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5
  D_other =~ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
  H_self =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_13+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15
  H_other =~ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19+dhf_endors_20
  F_self =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25
  F_other =~ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30
  
Level: 2 
  D =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5+ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
  H =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_13+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15+ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19+dhf_endors_20
  F =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25+ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30
')

six3factor.individual.testc.fit <- cfa(model = six3factor.model.testc,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(six3factor.individual.testc.fit, "output/six3factor.individual.testc.fit")
six3factor.individual.testc.fit <- readRDS("output/six3factor.individual.testc.fit")

summary(six3factor.individual.testc.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

six3factor.individual.testc.fitindices <- fitmeasures(six3factor.individual.testc.fit)
six3factor.individual.testc.params <- tidy(six3factor.individual.testc.fit)

Endorsement 6+3-Factor Individual Test Combined Remove 13

six3factor.model.testc.no13 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D_self =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5
  D_other =~ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
  H_self =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15
  H_other =~ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19+dhf_endors_20
  F_self =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25
  F_other =~ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30
  
Level: 2 
  D =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5+ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
  H =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15+ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19+dhf_endors_20
  F =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25+ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30
')

six3factor.model.testc.no13.fit <- cfa(model = six3factor.model.testc.no13,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(six3factor.model.testc.no13.fit, "output/six3factor.model.testc.no13.fit")
six3factor.model.testc.no13.fit <- readRDS("output/six3factor.model.testc.no13.fit")

summary(six3factor.model.testc.no13.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

six3factor.individual.testc.no13.fitindices <- fitmeasures(six3factor.model.testc.no13.fit)
six3factor.individual.testc.no13.params <- tidy(six3factor.model.testc.no13.fit)

Endorsement 6+3-Factor Individual Test Combined Remove 13 20

six3factor.model.testc.no20 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D_self =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5
  D_other =~ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
  H_self =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15
  H_other =~ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19
  F_self =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25
  F_other =~ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30
  
Level: 2 
  D =~ dhf_endors_1+dhf_endors_2+dhf_endors_3+dhf_endors_4+dhf_endors_5+ dhf_endors_6+dhf_endors_7+dhf_endors_8+dhf_endors_9+dhf_endors_10
  H =~ dhf_endors_11+dhf_endors_12+dhf_endors_14+dhf_endors_15+ dhf_endors_16+dhf_endors_17+dhf_endors_18+dhf_endors_19
  F =~ dhf_endors_21+dhf_endors_22+dhf_endors_23+dhf_endors_24+dhf_endors_25+ dhf_endors_26+dhf_endors_27+dhf_endors_28+dhf_endors_29+dhf_endors_30
')

six3factor.model.testc.no20.fit <- cfa(model = six3factor.model.testc.no20,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(six3factor.model.testc.no20.fit, "output/six3factor.model.testc.no20.fit")
six3factor.model.testc.no20.fit <- readRDS("output/six3factor.model.testc.no20.fit")

summary(six3factor.model.testc.no20.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

##               682.000                 0.000            187158.791 
##           baseline.df       baseline.pvalue                   cfi 
##               756.000                 0.000                 0.919 
##                   tli                  nnfi                   rfi 
##                 0.910                 0.910                 0.906 
##                   nfi                  pnfi                   ifi 
##                 0.915                 0.826                 0.919 
##                   rni                  logl     unrestricted.logl 
##                 0.919          -1059614.298          -1051705.568 
##                   aic                   bic                ntotal 
##           2119544.596           2120835.395             26098.000 
##                  bic2                 rmsea        rmsea.ci.lower 
##           2120333.275                 0.029                 0.029 
##        rmsea.ci.upper        rmsea.ci.level          rmsea.pvalue 
##                 0.030                 0.900                 1.000 
##        rmsea.close.h0 rmsea.notclose.pvalue     rmsea.notclose.h0 
##                 0.050                 0.000                 0.080 
##                  srmr           srmr_within          srmr_between 
##                 0.231                 0.039                 0.192
# pick a model based on the rules above

Norms Hierarchical Test Level 1

hierarchical.model.test1 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1
  D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
  
  D =~ D_self + D_other
  F =~ F_self + F_other
  H =~ H_self + H_other
  
Level: 2
', 
corr_norm)

hierarchical.norms.test1.fit <- cfa(model = hierarchical.model.test1,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full",
                    # options to consider
                    optim.method = "em"
                    # em.iter.max = 1000,
                    # em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    # em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )
beep()

saveRDS(hierarchical.norms.test1.fit, "output/hierarchical.norms.test1.fit")
# negative variance and not positive definite error 
hierarchical.norms.test1.fit <- readRDS("output/hierarchical.norms.test1.fit")

summary(hierarchical.norms.test1.fit, 
        fit.measures = TRUE, 
        rsquare = TRUE, 
        standardized = TRUE)

##    .dhf_norm_29       0.177    0.033    5.435    0.000    0.177    1.000
##    .dhf_norm_30       0.131    0.024    5.498    0.000    0.131    1.000
hierarchical.norms.test1.fitindices <- fitmeasures(hierarchical.norms.test1.fit)
hierarchical.norms.test1.params <- tidy(hierarchical.norms.test1.fit)

Norms Hierarchical Test Level 2

hierarchical.model.test2 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1', 
corr_norm, 
'
Level: 2 
  D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
  
  D =~ D_self + D_other
  F =~ F_self + F_other
  H =~ H_self + H_other
  
  D_self ~~ 0.01*D_self
  H_other ~~ 0.01*H_other
  D_other ~~ 0.01*D_other
')

hierarchical.norms.test2.fit <- cfa(model = hierarchical.model.test2,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

summary(hierarchical.norms.test2.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

saveRDS(hierarchical.norms.test2.fit, "output/hierarchical.norms.test2.fit")
hierarchical.norms.test2.fit <- readRDS("output/hierarchical.norms.test2.fit")

hierarchical.norms.test2.fitindices <- fitmeasures(hierarchical.norms.test2.fit)
hierarchical.norms.test2.params <- tidy(hierarchical.norms.test2.fit)

Norms 3-factor Test Level 1

threefactor.model.test1 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1
  D =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5+ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15+ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25+ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30

Level: 2
', 
corr_norm)

threefactor.norms.test1.fit <- cfa(model = threefactor.model.test1,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full",
                    # options to consider
                    optim.method = "em", 
                    em.iter.max = 1000,
                    em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(threefactor.norms.test1.fit, "output/threefactor.norms.test1.fit")
# model did not converge
threefactor.norms.test1.fit <- readRDS("output/threefactor.norms.test1.fit")

summary(threefactor.norms.test1.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

# threefactor.norms.test1.fitindices <- fitmeasures(threefactor.norms.test1.fit)
threefactor.norms.test1.params <- tidy(threefactor.norms.test1.fit)

Norms 3-Factor Test Level 2

threefactor.model.test2 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1', 
corr_norm, 
'
Level: 2 
   D =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5+ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
   H =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15+ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
   F =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25+ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30

')

threefactor.norms.test2.fit <- cfa(model = threefactor.model.test2,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(threefactor.norms.test2.fit, "output/threefactor.norms.test2.fit")
threefactor.norms.test2.fit <- readRDS("output/threefactor.norms.test2.fit")

summary(threefactor.norms.test2.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

##     dhf_norm_30       0.841
threefactor.norms.test2.fitindices <- fitmeasures(threefactor.norms.test2.fit)
threefactor.norms.test2.params <- tidy(threefactor.norms.test2.fit)

Norms 6-factor Test Level 1

sixfactor.model.test1 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1
  D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30

Level: 2
', 
corr_norm)

sixfactor.norms.test1.fit <- cfa(model = sixfactor.model.test1,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full",
                    # options to consider
                    optim.method = "em", 
                    em.iter.max = 1000,
                    em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(sixfactor.norms.test1.fit, "output/sixfactor.norms.test1.fit")
# model did not converge
sixfactor.norms.test1.fit <- readRDS("output/sixfactor.norms.test1.fit")

summary(sixfactor.norms.test1.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

# sixfactor.norms.test1.fitindices <- fitmeasures(sixfactor.norms.test1.fit)
sixfactor.norms.test1.params <- tidy(sixfactor.norms.test1.fit)

Norms 6-Factor Test Level 2

sixfactor.model.test2 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1', 
corr_norm, 
'
Level: 2 
  D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30

')

sixfactor.norms.test2.fit <- cfa(model = sixfactor.model.test2,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(sixfactor.norms.test2.fit, "output/sixfactor.norms.test2.fit")
sixfactor.norms.test2.fit <- readRDS("output/sixfactor.norms.test2.fit")

summary(sixfactor.norms.test2.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 346 iterations
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                       570
## 
##                                                   Used       Total
##   Number of observations                         23419       26479
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63            
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                                       
##   Test statistic                              1087.153
##   Degrees of freedom                               390
##   P-value (Chi-square)                           0.000
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                            301400.456
##   Degrees of freedom                               870
##   P-value                                        0.000
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    0.998
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       0.995
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)           -1004555.850
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)   -1004012.274
##                                                       
##   Akaike (AIC)                             2010251.701
##   Bayesian (BIC)                           2014846.644
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)    2013035.202
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.009
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.008
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.009
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                    1.000
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                    0.000
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual (corr metric):
## 
##   SRMR (within covariance matrix)                0.000
##   SRMR (between covariance matrix)               0.182
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                             Standard
##   Information                                 Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## 
## Level 1 [within]:
## 
## Covariances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##  .dhf_norm_1 ~~

sixfactor.norms.test2.fitindices <- fitmeasures(sixfactor.norms.test2.fit)
sixfactor.norms.test2.params <- tidy(sixfactor.norms.test2.fit)

Norms Hierarchical Test Combined

hierarchical.model.testc <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1
  D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
  
  D =~ D_self + D_other
  F =~ F_self + F_other
  H =~ H_self + H_other
  
  D_other ~~ 0.01*D_other
  
Level: 2
 D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
  
  D =~ D_self + D_other
  F =~ F_self + F_other
  H =~ H_self + H_other
  
  H_other ~~ 0.01*H_other
  D_other ~~ 0.01*D_other

')

hierarchical.model.testc.fit <- cfa(model = hierarchical.model.testc,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full",
                    # options to consider
                    optim.method = "em"
                    # em.iter.max = 1000,
                    # em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    # em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )
beep()

saveRDS(hierarchical.model.testc.fit, "output/hierarchical.model.testc.fit")
hierarchical.model.testc.fit <- readRDS("output/hierarchical.model.testc.fit")

summary(hierarchical.model.testc.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

hierarchical.norms.testc.fitindices <- fitmeasures(hierarchical.model.testc.fit)
hierarchical.model.testc.params <- tidy(hierarchical.model.testc.fit)

Norms 6-Factor Test Combined

sixfactor.model.testc <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
  
Level: 2 
  D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30

')

sixfactor.norms.testc.fit <- cfa(model = sixfactor.model.testc,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(sixfactor.norms.testc.fit, "output/sixfactor.norms.testc.fit")
sixfactor.norms.testc.fit <- readRDS("output/sixfactor.norms.testc.fit")

summary(sixfactor.norms.testc.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 359 iterations
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                       180
##

##     dhf_norm_30       0.881
sixfactor.norms.testc.fitindices <- fitmeasures(sixfactor.norms.testc.fit)
sixfactor.norms.testc.params <- tidy(sixfactor.norms.testc.fit)

Norms 3-Factor Test Combined

threefactor.model.testc <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5+ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
   H =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15+ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
   F =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25+ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30


Level: 2 
   D =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5+ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
   H =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15+ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
   F =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25+ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30

')

threefactor.norms.testc.fit <- cfa(model = threefactor.model.testc,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(threefactor.norms.testc.fit, "output/threefactor.norms.testc.fit")
threefactor.norms.testc.fit <- readRDS("output/threefactor.norms.testc.fit")

summary(threefactor.norms.testc.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 341 iterations
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                       156
## 
##                                                   Used       Total
##   Number of observations                         23419       26479
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63            
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                                        
##   Test statistic                              49427.703
##   Degrees of freedom                                804
##   P-value (Chi-square)                            0.000
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                            301400.456
##   Degrees of freedom                               870
##   P-value                                        0.000
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    0.838
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       0.825
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)           -1028726.125
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)   -1004012.274
##                                                       
##   Akaike (AIC)                             2057764.251
##   Bayesian (BIC)                           2059021.814

##     dhf_norm_30       0.839
threefactor.norms.testc.fitindices <- fitmeasures(threefactor.norms.testc.fit)
threefactor.norms.testc.params <- tidy(threefactor.norms.testc.fit)

Norms 6+3-Factor Test Combined

six3factor.model.testc <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
  
Level: 2 
  D =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5+ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_13+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15+ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25+ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
')

six3factor.norms.testc.fit <- cfa(model = six3factor.model.testc,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(six3factor.norms.testc.fit, "output/six3factor.norms.testc.fit")
six3factor.norms.testc.fit <- readRDS("output/six3factor.norms.testc.fit")

summary(six3factor.norms.testc.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 424 iterations
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                       168
## 
##                                                   Used       Total
##   Number of observations                         23419       26479
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63            
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                                        
##   Test statistic                              31080.183
##   Degrees of freedom                                792
##   P-value (Chi-square)                            0.000
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                            301400.456
##   Degrees of freedom                               870
##   P-value                                        0.000
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    0.899
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       0.889
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)           -1019552.366
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)   -1004012.274

six3factor.norms.testc.fitindices <- fitmeasures(six3factor.norms.testc.fit)
six3factor.norms.testc.params <- tidy(six3factor.norms.testc.fit)

Norms 6+3-Factor Test Combined Modified Remove 13

six3factor.model.no13.testc <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
  
Level: 2 
  D =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5+ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15+ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19+dhf_norm_20
  F =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25+ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
')

six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fit <- cfa(model = six3factor.model.testc.no13,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fit, "output/six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fit")
six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fit <- readRDS("output/six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fit")

summary(six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

##     dhf_endors_19     0.579
##     dhf_endors_20     0.082
##     dhf_endors_21     0.777
##     dhf_endors_22     0.443
##     dhf_endors_23     0.797
##     dhf_endors_24     0.684
##     dhf_endors_25     0.817
##     dhf_endors_26     0.533
##     dhf_endors_27     0.847
##     dhf_endors_28     0.406
##     dhf_endors_29     0.619
##     dhf_endors_30     0.665
six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fitindices <- fitmeasures(six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fit)
six3factor.norms.testc.no13.params <- tidy(six3factor.norms.testc.no13.fit)

Norms 6+3-Factor Test Combined Modified Remove 13 + 20

six3factor.model.testc.no20 <- 
paste0(' 
Level: 1

D_self =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5
  D_other =~ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H_self =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15
  H_other =~ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19
  F_self =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25
  F_other =~ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
  
Level: 2 
  D =~ dhf_norm_1+dhf_norm_2+dhf_norm_3+dhf_norm_4+dhf_norm_5+ dhf_norm_6+dhf_norm_7+dhf_norm_8+dhf_norm_9+dhf_norm_10
  H =~ dhf_norm_11+dhf_norm_12+dhf_norm_14+dhf_norm_15+ dhf_norm_16+dhf_norm_17+dhf_norm_18+dhf_norm_19
  F =~ dhf_norm_21+dhf_norm_22+dhf_norm_23+dhf_norm_24+dhf_norm_25+ dhf_norm_26+dhf_norm_27+dhf_norm_28+dhf_norm_29+dhf_norm_30
')

six3factor.norms.testc.no20.fit <- cfa(model = six3factor.model.testc.no20,
                    data = dt4,
                    cluster = "country_res_full"
                    # options to consider
                    #optim.method = "em", 
                    #em.iter.max = 1000
                    #em.fx.tol = 1e-08, 
                    #em.dx.tol = 1e-04
                    )

saveRDS(six3factor.norms.testc.no20.fit, "output/six3factor.norms.testc.no20.fit")
six3factor.norms.testc.no20.fit <- readRDS("output/six3factor.norms.testc.no20.fit")

summary(six3factor.norms.testc.no20.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

six3factor.norms.testc.no20.params <- tidy(six3factor.norms.testc.no20.fit)

# pick a model based on the rules above

Measurement invariance via alignment optimization

dt4$cluster <- recode_factor(dt4$country_res_full, 
                             "Argentina" = "Latin America",
                             "Brazil" = "Latin America",
                             "Chile" = "Latin America",
                             "Mexico" = "Latin America",
                             "Armenia" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Bosnia-Herzegovina" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Bulgaria" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Croatia" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Czech Republic" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Georgia" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Greece" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Hungary" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Kosovo" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "North Macedonia" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Poland" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Russia" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Serbia" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Slovakia" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Kazakhstan" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Ukraine" = "Eastern Europe",
                             "Australia" = "Anglo-Saxon",
                             "Canada" = "Anglo-Saxon",
                             "New Zealand" = "Anglo-Saxon",
                             "Ireland" = "Anglo-Saxon",
                             "United Kingdom" = "Anglo-Saxon",
                             "United States" = "Anglo-Saxon",
                             "Austria" = "Germanic",
                             "Germany" = "Germanic",
                             "Switzerland" = "Germanic",
                             "The Netherlands" = "Germanic",
                             "Bangladesh" = "South-East Asia",
                             "India" = "South-East Asia",
                             "Indonesia" = "South-East Asia",
                             "Iran" = "South-East Asia",
                             "Malaysia" = "South-East Asia",
                             "Pakistan" = "South-East Asia",
                             "Philippines" = "South-East Asia",
                             "Thailand" = "South-East Asia",
                             "Cameroon" = "Africa",
                             "Ethiopia" = "Africa",
                             "Kenya" = "Africa",
                             "Namibia" = "Africa",
                             "Nigeria" = "Africa",
                             "South Africa" = "Africa",
                             "Ghana" = "Africa",
                             "China" = "Confucian",
                             "Japan" = "Confucian",
                             "Singapore" = "Confucian",
                             "Hong Kong" = "Confucian",
                             "Taiwan" = "Confucian",
                             "Vietnam" = "Confucian",
                             "France" = "Latin Europe",
                             "Israel" = "Latin Europe",
                             "Italy" = "Latin Europe",
                             "Portugal" = "Latin Europe",
                             "Romania" = "Latin Europe",
                             "Spain" = "Latin Europe",
                             "Kuwait" = "Middle East",
                             "Morocco" = "Middle East",
                             "Saudi Arabia" = "Middle East",
                             "Lebanon" = "Middle East",
                             "Turkey" = "Middle East",
                             "Uzbekistan" = "Middle East",
                             "United Arab Emirates" = "Middle East")

dt4[dt4 == -99] <- NA

MGCFA Endorsement

endorse.model.configural <- '
D_self =~ dhf_endors_1 + dhf_endors_2 + dhf_endors_3 + dhf_endors_4 + dhf_endors_5
D_other =~ dhf_endors_6 + dhf_endors_7 + dhf_endors_8 + dhf_endors_9 + dhf_endors_10
H_self =~ dhf_endors_11 + dhf_endors_12 + dhf_endors_13 + dhf_endors_14 + dhf_endors_15
H_other =~ dhf_endors_16 + dhf_endors_17 + dhf_endors_18 + dhf_endors_19 + dhf_endors_20
F_self =~ dhf_endors_21 + dhf_endors_22 + dhf_endors_23 + dhf_endors_24 + dhf_endors_25
F_other =~ dhf_endors_26 + dhf_endors_27 + dhf_endors_28 + dhf_endors_29 + dhf_endors_30
'

endorse.model.configural.fit <- cfa(
  model = endorse.model.configural,
  data = dt4,
  group = "cluster",
  estimator = "ML",
  std.lv = FALSE,      # <- critical to match Mplus
  missing = "fiml"
)

##    25709 25710 25711 25807.
saveRDS(endorse.model.configural.fit, "output/endorse.model.configural.fit")
endorse.model.configural.fit <- readRDS("output/endorse.model.configural.fit")

summary(endorse.model.configural.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

##                    Estimate
##     dhf_endors_1      0.482
##     dhf_endors_2      0.148

##     dhf_endors_22     0.243
##     dhf_endors_23     0.341

##     dhf_endors_25     0.471
##     dhf_endors_26     0.224
##     dhf_endors_27     0.558
##     dhf_endors_28     0.199
##     dhf_endors_29     0.282
##     dhf_endors_30     0.261
endorse.model.configural.fitindex <- fitmeasures(endorse.model.configural.fit)
endorse.model.configural.params <- tidy(endorse.model.configural.fit)

MGCFA Endorsement Alignment

endorse.items <- paste0("dhf_endors_", 1:30)

align.endorse.prep <- sirt::invariance_alignment_cfa_config(
  dat   = dt4[, endorse.items],
  group = dt4$cluster,
  model = endorse.model.configural
)

lambda <- align.endorse.prep$lambda
nu     <- align.endorse.prep$nu
Ng     <- align.endorse.prep$N
wgt <- matrix(sqrt(Ng), length(Ng), ncol(nu))

endorse.res <- sirt::invariance.alignment(
  lambda = lambda,
  nu     = nu,
  wgt    = wgt,
  align.pow = c(2,2),
  eps = .01,
  meth = 3
)

saveRDS(endorse.res, "output/endorse.alignment.output")
endorse.res <- readRDS("output/endorse.alignment.output")

summary(endorse.res)

## Summary Absolute Residuals Loadings Lambda 
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##       0       0       0       0       0       0 
## -----------------------------------------------------------------
## Summary Absolute Residuals Intercepts Nu 
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   0.001   0.115   0.228   0.267   0.359   1.048

MGCFA Norms

norms.model.configural <- '
D_self =~ dhf_norm_1 + dhf_norm_2 + dhf_norm_3 + dhf_norm_4 + dhf_norm_5
D_other =~ dhf_norm_6 + dhf_norm_7 + dhf_norm_8 + dhf_norm_9 + dhf_norm_10
H_self =~ dhf_norm_11 + dhf_norm_12 + dhf_norm_13 + dhf_norm_14 + dhf_norm_15
H_other =~ dhf_norm_16 + dhf_norm_17 + dhf_norm_18 + dhf_norm_19 + dhf_norm_20
F_self =~ dhf_norm_21 + dhf_norm_22 + dhf_norm_23 + dhf_norm_24 + dhf_norm_25
F_other =~ dhf_norm_26 + dhf_norm_27 + dhf_norm_28 + dhf_norm_29 + dhf_norm_30
'

norms.model.configural.fit <- cfa(
  model = norms.model.configural,
  data = dt4,
  group = "cluster",
  estimator = "ML",
  std.lv = FALSE,      # <- critical to match Mplus
  missing = "fiml"
)

saveRDS(norms.model.configural.fit, "output/norms.model.configural.fit")
norms.model.configural.fit <- readRDS("output/norms.model.configural.fit")

summary(norms.model.configural.fit,
        fit.measures = TRUE,
        rsquare = TRUE,
        standardized = TRUE)

## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     dhf_norm_1        0.520
##     dhf_norm_2        0.356
##     dhf_norm_3        0.742

##     dhf_norm_8        0.736

##     dhf_norm_18       0.600
##     dhf_norm_19       0.606
##     dhf_norm_20       0.548

##     dhf_norm_25       0.570
##     dhf_norm_26       0.400
##     dhf_norm_27       0.434
##     dhf_norm_28       0.456
##     dhf_norm_29       0.108
##     dhf_norm_30       0.278
norms.model.configural.fitindex <- fitmeasures(norms.model.configural.fit)
norms.model.configural.params <- tidy(norms.model.configural.fit)

MGCFA Norms Alignment

norm.items <- paste0("dhf_norm_", 1:30)

align.norm.prep <- sirt::invariance_alignment_cfa_config(
  dat   = dt4[, norm.items],
  group = dt4$cluster,
  model = endorse.model.configural
)

lambda <- align.norm.prep$lambda
nu     <- align.norm.prep$nu
Ng     <- align.norm.prep$N
wgt <- matrix(sqrt(Ng), length(Ng), ncol(nu))

norms.res <- sirt::invariance.alignment(
  lambda = lambda,
  nu     = nu,
  wgt    = wgt,
  align.pow = c(2, 2),
  eps = .01,
  meth = 3
)

saveRDS(norms.res, "output/norms.alignment.output")
norms.res <- readRDS("output/norms.alignment.output")

summary(norms.res)

##   0.000   0.082   0.148   0.180   0.256   0.729

Main study: DHF scores across the world

Figure 1 shows the prevalence of cultural logics of dignity, honor, and face across 64 countries of the world. Table Sxx shows DHF endorsement and norms scores in each sample and by world regions. Across all countries, people endorsed dignity more strongly than honor or face. There were, however, meaningful differences across the world regions: dignity was endorsed most strongly the most in Germanic countries and least stronglythe least in Africa; honor was endorsed most stronglythe most in the Middle East and Eastern Europe, and least stronglythe least in Germanic countries; face was endorsed most stronglythe most in Africa and Confucian countries, and least stronglythe least in Germanic countries and Latin America. The own endorsement scores and the perceived norms diverged: when looking at the global distribution of the six facets, people across the world endorsed dignity-other and dignity-self most strongly and face-other and honor-self least strongly, but perceived that others around them endorsed honor-other and face-self most and dignity-other least.

# calculate participant means for DHF endorsement
dt4 <- dt4  %>% 
  mutate(
    D_self_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_1:dhf_endors_5), na.rm=T),
    D_other_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_6:dhf_endors_10),na.rm=T),
    H_self_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_11:dhf_endors_15),na.rm=T),
    H_other_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_16:dhf_endors_20),na.rm=T),
    F_self_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_21:dhf_endors_25),na.rm=T),
    F_other_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_26:dhf_endors_30),na.rm=T),
    D_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_1:dhf_endors_10),na.rm=T),
    H_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_11:dhf_endors_20),na.rm=T),
    F_end = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_21:dhf_endors_30),na.rm=T)
  )
# endorsement corrected for response style (within-participant centering)
dt4 <- dt4  %>% 
  mutate(dhf_endors_mean = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_endors_1:dhf_endors_30), na.rm=T))

dt4 <- dt4  %>% 
  mutate(
    D_self_endc = D_self_end - dhf_endors_mean,
    D_other_endc = D_other_end - dhf_endors_mean,
    H_self_endc = H_self_end - dhf_endors_mean,
    H_other_endc = H_other_end - dhf_endors_mean,
    F_self_endc = F_self_end - dhf_endors_mean,
    F_other_endc = F_other_end - dhf_endors_mean,
    D_endc = D_end - dhf_endors_mean,
    H_endc = H_end - dhf_endors_mean,
    F_endc = F_end - dhf_endors_mean,
  )

# calculate country means for endorsement
country_endors <- dt4 %>%
  group_by(country_res_full) %>%
  summarize(
    across(
      c(D_self_end, D_other_end, H_self_end, H_other_end, F_self_end, F_other_end, D_end, H_end, F_end, D_self_endc, D_other_endc, H_self_endc, H_other_endc, F_self_endc, F_other_endc, D_endc, H_endc, F_endc),
      ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
      .names = "{.col}_mean_country"
    )
  )

dt4 <- dt4 %>%
  left_join(country_endors, by = "country_res_full")
# calculate participant means for DHF norms 
dt4 <- dt4  %>% 
  mutate(
    D_self_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_1:dhf_norm_5), na.rm=T),
    D_other_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_6:dhf_norm_10),na.rm=T),
    H_self_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_11:dhf_norm_15),na.rm=T),
    H_other_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_16:dhf_norm_20),na.rm=T),
    F_self_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_21:dhf_norm_25),na.rm=T),
    F_other_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_26:dhf_norm_30),na.rm=T),
    D_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_1:dhf_norm_10),na.rm=T),
    H_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_11:dhf_norm_20),na.rm=T),
    F_norm = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_21:dhf_norm_30),na.rm=T)
  )

# endorsement corrected for response style (within-participant centering)
dt4 <- dt4  %>% 
  mutate(dhf_norm_mean = rowMeans(select(.,dhf_norm_1:dhf_norm_30), na.rm=T))

dt4 <- dt4  %>% 
  mutate(
    D_self_normc = D_self_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
    D_other_normc = D_other_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
    H_self_normc = H_self_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
    H_other_normc = H_other_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
    F_self_normc = F_self_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
    F_other_normc = F_other_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
    D_normc = D_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
    H_normc = H_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
    F_normc = F_norm - dhf_norm_mean,
  )

# calculate country means for endorsement
country_norm <- dt4 %>%
  group_by(country_res_full) %>%
  summarize(
    across(
      c(D_self_norm, D_other_norm, H_self_norm, H_other_norm, F_self_norm, F_other_norm, D_norm, H_norm, F_norm, D_self_normc, D_other_normc, H_self_normc, H_other_normc, F_self_normc, F_other_normc, D_normc, H_normc, F_normc),
      ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
      .names = "{.col}_mean_country"
    )
  )

dt4 <- dt4 %>%
  left_join(country_norm, by = "country_res_full")

DHF endorsement and norms scores (centered) across world regions

dt4 %>% 
  select(
    D_self_end, D_other_end, H_self_end, H_other_end, F_self_end, F_other_end, D_end, H_end, F_end, 
    D_self_endc:F_endc, D_self_normc:F_normc, cluster) %>% 
  group_by(cluster) %>% 
  summarize(
    across(
      c(D_self_end, D_other_end, H_self_end, H_other_end, F_self_end, F_other_end, D_end, H_end, F_end, D_self_endc, D_other_endc, H_self_endc, H_other_endc, F_self_endc, F_other_endc, D_endc, H_endc, F_endc),
      ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
      .names = "{.col}_mean_region"
    ),
    across(
      c(D_self_end, D_other_end, H_self_end, H_other_end, F_self_end, F_other_end, D_end, H_end, F_end, D_self_endc, D_other_endc, H_self_endc, H_other_endc, F_self_endc, F_other_endc, D_endc, H_endc, F_endc),
      ~sd(., na.rm = TRUE),
      .names = "{.col}_sd_region"
    )
  )
## # A tibble: 10 × 37
##    cluster  D_self_end_mean_region D_other_end_mean_reg…¹ H_self_end_mean_region
##    <fct>                     <dbl>                  <dbl>                  <dbl>
##  1 Latin A…                   5.14                   5.51                   3.49
##  2 Eastern…                   5.05                   5.05                   3.79
##  3 Anglo-S…                   5.01                   5.14                   3.52
##  4 Germanic                   5.03                   5.37                   3.18
##  5 South-E…                   5.03                   5.18                   4.04
##  6 Africa                     4.94                   5.15                   4.19
##  7 Confuci…                   4.56                   5.00                   3.62
##  8 Latin E…                   4.98                   5.29                   3.52
##  9 Middle …                   5.09                   5.01                   4.30
## 10 Rwanda                     4.79                   4.79                   3.73
## # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​D_other_end_mean_region
## # ℹ 33 more variables: H_other_end_mean_region <dbl>,
## #   F_self_end_mean_region <dbl>, F_other_end_mean_region <dbl>,
## #   D_end_mean_region <dbl>, H_end_mean_region <dbl>, F_end_mean_region <dbl>,
## #   D_self_endc_mean_region <dbl>, D_other_endc_mean_region <dbl>,
## #   H_self_endc_mean_region <dbl>, H_other_endc_mean_region <dbl>,
## #   F_self_endc_mean_region <dbl>, F_other_endc_mean_region <dbl>, …

DHF endorsement and norms scores (centered) across countries

country_endors
## # A tibble: 64 × 19
##    country_res_full   D_self_end_mean_country D_other_end_mean_country

##  1 Argentina                             5.22                     5.51
##  2 Armenia                               4.97                     4.93
##  3 Australia                             5.01                     5.18
##  4 Austria                               4.97                     5.30
##  5 Bangladesh                            5.18                     5.27
##  6 Bosnia-Herzegovina                    5.17                     5.17
##  7 Brazil                                5.04                     5.53

## 10 Canada                                5.01                     5.13

## #   H_other_end_mean_country <dbl>, F_self_end_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   F_other_end_mean_country <dbl>, D_end_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   H_end_mean_country <dbl>, F_end_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   D_self_endc_mean_country <dbl>, D_other_endc_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   H_self_endc_mean_country <dbl>, H_other_endc_mean_country <dbl>, …
country_norm
## # A tibble: 64 × 19
##    country_res_full   D_self_norm_mean_country D_other_norm_mean_country
##    <fct>                                 <dbl>                     <dbl>
##  1 Argentina                              4.65                      4.33
##  2 Armenia                                3.57                      3.36
##  3 Australia                              4.55                      4.43
##  4 Austria                                4.43                      4.3 
##  5 Bangladesh                             4.94                      4.96
##  6 Bosnia-Herzegovina                     3.75                      3.50
##  7 Brazil                                 4.33                      4.21
##  8 Bulgaria                               4.19                      3.54
##  9 Cameroon                               4.81                      4.88
## 10 Canada                                 4.53                      4.38
## # ℹ 54 more rows
## # ℹ 16 more variables: H_self_norm_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   H_other_norm_mean_country <dbl>, F_self_norm_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   F_other_norm_mean_country <dbl>, D_norm_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   H_norm_mean_country <dbl>, F_norm_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   D_self_normc_mean_country <dbl>, D_other_normc_mean_country <dbl>,
## #   H_self_normc_mean_country <dbl>, H_other_normc_mean_country <dbl>, …

Visualizations

Cleveland dot plot of Honor/Face/Dignity norms by country (non-centered)

# 1) Reshape to long format (one row per country × dimension)
df_long <- country_norm %>%
  select(
    country_res_full,
    Honor   = H_norm_mean_country,
    Face    = F_norm_mean_country,
    Dignity = D_norm_mean_country
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(Honor:Dignity, names_to = "Dimension", values_to = "Score")

# 2) Choose how to order countries on the y-axis
#    Option A: by overall mean across the 3 dimensions (lowest → highest)
country_order <- df_long %>%
  group_by(country_res_full) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(Score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(mean_score) %>%
  pull(country_res_full)

#    (Alternative)
#    # Option B: by Honor only
#    country_order <- df_long %>% filter(Dimension == "Honor") %>%
#      arrange(Score) %>% pull(country_res_full)
#
#    # Option C: alphabetical
#    country_order <- sort(unique(df_long$country_res_full))

# 3) Apply the order so ggplot respects it
df_long$country_res_full <- factor(df_long$country_res_full, levels = country_order)

# 4) Precompute per-country ranges (min→max across the three dimensions)
ranges <- df_long %>%
  group_by(country_res_full) %>%
  summarise(
    xmin = min(Score, na.rm = TRUE),
    xmax = max(Score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 5) Plot
p <- ggplot() +
  # grey range bars (min→max per country)
  geom_segment(
    data = ranges,
    aes(x = xmin, xend = xmax, y = country_res_full, yend = country_res_full),
    color = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  # colored points for the three dimensions
  geom_point(
    data = df_long,
    aes(x = Score, y = country_res_full, color = Dimension),
    size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8,
    # Use small vertical jitter if overlapping points are an issue; keep width at 0 for x
    position = position_jitter(height = 0, width = 0)
    # If you prefer perfectly aligned dots, use: position = position_identity()
  ) +
  # Color palette: Dark2 is colorblind-friendly; remove legend title
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2", name = NULL) +
  labs(x = "Societal mean (1-7)", y = NULL, title = NULL) +
  # Minimal theme + enforce true white background
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.background     = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y  = element_blank(),     # cleaner y axis
    axis.text.y         = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position     = "top"
  )

p

# 6) Save
#    Adjust height if you have many countries (more rows → more height).
ggsave(
  filename = "figures/DHF_Norms_dotplot_noncentered.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 9, height = 14, dpi = 600
)

# Vector version for journals (crisp text/lines):
ggsave(
  filename = "figures/DHF_Norms_dotplot_noncentered.pdf",
  plot = p,
  width = 9, height = 14
)

Cleveland dot plot of Honor/Face/Dignity norms by country (non-centered & centered)

# 1) Long data for NON-centered
df_nc <- country_norm %>%
  select(
    country_res_full,
    Honor   = H_norm_mean_country,
    Face    = F_norm_mean_country,
    Dignity = D_norm_mean_country
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(Honor:Dignity, names_to = "Dimension", values_to = "Score") %>%
  mutate(Centering = "Non-centered")

# 2) Long data for CENTERED (same dimension names)
df_c <- country_norm %>%
  select(
    country_res_full,
    Honor   = H_normc_mean_country,
    Face    = F_normc_mean_country,
    Dignity = D_normc_mean_country
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(Honor:Dignity, names_to = "Dimension", values_to = "Score") %>%
  mutate(Centering = "Centered")

# 3) Combine + set factor orders
df_both <- bind_rows(df_nc, df_c) %>%
  mutate(
    Dimension = factor(Dimension, levels = c("Honor","Face","Dignity")),
    Centering = factor(Centering, levels = c("Non-centered","Centered"))
  )

# 4) Fix a single country order (so both facets share the same y-axis)
#    Here: order by the NON-centered average across the 3 dimensions (intuitive baseline)
country_order <- df_nc %>%
  group_by(country_res_full) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(Score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(mean_score) %>%
  pull(country_res_full)

df_both$country_res_full <- factor(df_both$country_res_full, levels = country_order)

# 5) (Optional) draw per-country range segments across the 3 dimensions *within each facet*
ranges <- df_both %>%
  group_by(Centering, country_res_full) %>%
  summarise(
    xmin = min(Score, na.rm = TRUE),
    xmax = max(Score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 6) Plot: one panel per Centering; dots colored by Dimension
set.seed(123)  # reproducible jitter

p <- ggplot() +
  # grey connector showing spread across the three dimensions for each country in each facet
  geom_segment(
    data = ranges,
    aes(x = xmin, xend = xmax, y = country_res_full, yend = country_res_full),
    color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  # three dots per country (Honor/Face/Dignity)
  geom_point(
    data = df_both,
    aes(x = Score, y = country_res_full, color = Dimension),
    size = 2.5, alpha = 0.75,
    position = position_jitter(height = 0, width = 0)  # small vertical separation
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Centering, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +  # free_x lets centered vs non-centered have their own scales
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2", name = NULL) +
  labs(x = "Societal mean (1-7)", y = NULL, title = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position    = "top"
  )

p

# 7) Save (increase height for many countries)
ggsave("figures/DHF_Norms_dotplot.png", p,
       width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 600)
ggsave("figures/DHF_Norms_dotplot.pdf", p,
       width = 18, height = 14)

World map of Honor/Face/Dignity norms (non-centered scores)

# --- 1) World polygons (drop Antarctica for cleaner view) ---
# Uses Natural Earth country names for joining. Currently in geographic (WGS84) CRS.
# If you want Robinson, add: world <- st_transform(world, "+proj=robin") after this block.
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") |>
  select(name, geometry) |>
  filter(name != "Antarctica")

# --- 2) Rename and recode to EXACT Natural Earth names ---
# Harmonize your dataset's country labels to match Natural Earth.
# Add more entries to recode() as mismatches arise.
country_data <- country_norm |>
  transmute(
    name    = country_res_full,
    Honor   = H_norm_mean_country,
    Face    = F_norm_mean_country,
    Dignity = D_norm_mean_country
  ) |>
  mutate(
    name = dplyr::recode(
      name,
      "Bosnia-Herzegovina" = "Bosnia and Herzegovina",
      "Czech Republic"     = "Czechia",
      "The Netherlands"    = "Netherlands",
      "United States"      = "United States of America"
    )
  )

# --- 3) Join to polygons ---
# Left join keeps all polygons; countries without data will map as NA (filled by na.value).
world_join <- world |> left_join(country_data, by = "name")

# (Optional) quickly see if any still unmatched
# Use this to catch remaining naming issues and extend recode() above.
# anti_join(country_data, st_drop_geometry(world), by = "name") |> distinct(name)

# --- 4) Long form + consistent limits across facets (so colors are comparable) ---
# Non-centered scores: keep a single limit across all three to make colors comparable row-wise.
world_long <- world_join |>
  select(geometry, Honor, Face, Dignity) |>
  pivot_longer(c(Honor, Face, Dignity),
               names_to = "Dimension", values_to = "Score") |>
  mutate(Dimension = factor(Dimension, levels = c("Honor","Face","Dignity")))

# Set limits explicitly for reproducibility; alternatively use range(world_long$Score, na.rm=TRUE).
lims <- c(3,5.5) # range(world_long$Score, na.rm = TRUE)

# --- 5) Plot: titles centered, thin white borders, compact legend ---
# Apply the same basic theme to all six maps; legends will later be collected per row.
common_theme <- theme(
  plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"),
  legend.position = "bottom",
  legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
  legend.text  = element_text(size = 9)
)

# Non-centered maps (viridis option "C")
p_honor <- ggplot(filter(world_long, Dimension == "Honor")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "D",
                     limits = lims, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Honor norms") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

p_face <- ggplot(filter(world_long, Dimension == "Face")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "D",
                     limits = lims, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Face norms") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

p_dignity <- ggplot(filter(world_long, Dimension == "Dignity")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "D",
                     limits = lims, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Dignity norms") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

# ---- build plot for non-centered scores only ----
p <- (p_honor | p_face | p_dignity) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
p

# Export plot
ggsave("figures/DHF_Norms_worldmap_noncentered.png", p,
       width = 18, height = 4, dpi = 600)
ggsave("figures/DHF_Norms_worldmap_noncentered.pdf", p,
       width = 18, height = 4)

World map of Honor/Face/Dignity norms

# --- 1) World polygons (drop Antarctica for cleaner view) ---
# Uses Natural Earth country names for joining. Currently in geographic (WGS84) CRS.
# If you want Robinson, add: world <- st_transform(world, "+proj=robin") after this block.
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") |>
  select(name, geometry) |>
  filter(name != "Antarctica")

# --- 2) Rename and recode to EXACT Natural Earth names ---
# Harmonize your dataset's country labels to match Natural Earth.
# Add more entries to recode() as mismatches arise.
country_data <- country_norm |>
  transmute(
    name    = country_res_full,
    Honor   = H_norm_mean_country,
    Face    = F_norm_mean_country,
    Dignity = D_norm_mean_country,
    HonorC   = H_normc_mean_country,
    FaceC    = F_normc_mean_country,
    DignityC = D_normc_mean_country,
  ) |>
  mutate(
    name = dplyr::recode(
      name,
      "Bosnia-Herzegovina" = "Bosnia and Herzegovina",
      "Czech Republic"     = "Czechia",
      "The Netherlands"    = "Netherlands",
      "United States"      = "United States of America"
    )
  )

# --- 3) Join to polygons ---
# Left join keeps all polygons; countries without data will map as NA (filled by na.value).
world_join <- world |> left_join(country_data, by = "name")

# (Optional) quickly see if any still unmatched
# Use this to catch remaining naming issues and extend recode() above.
# anti_join(country_data, st_drop_geometry(world), by = "name") |> distinct(name)

# --- 4) Long form + consistent limits across facets (so colors are comparable) ---
# Non-centered scores: keep a single limit across all three to make colors comparable row-wise.
world_long <- world_join |>
  select(geometry, Honor, Face, Dignity) |>
  pivot_longer(c(Honor, Face, Dignity),
               names_to = "Dimension", values_to = "Score") |>
  mutate(Dimension = factor(Dimension, levels = c("Honor","Face","Dignity")))

# Set limits explicitly for reproducibility; alternatively use range(world_long$Score, na.rm=TRUE).
lims <- c(3,5.5) # range(world_long$Score, na.rm = TRUE)

# Centered scores: keep them separate (different palette/legend) and use symmetric limits if possible.
world_long_centered <- world_join |>
  select(geometry, HonorC, FaceC, DignityC) |>
  pivot_longer(c(HonorC, FaceC, DignityC),
               names_to = "Dimension", values_to = "Score") |>
  mutate(Dimension = factor(Dimension, levels = c("HonorC","FaceC","DignityC")))

# Prefer symmetric bounds around 0 for interpretability (edit as needed).
limsc <- c(-1,1) # range(world_long_centered$Score, na.rm = TRUE)

# --- 5) Plot: titles centered, thin white borders, compact legend ---
# Apply the same basic theme to all six maps; legends will later be collected per row.
common_theme <- theme(
  plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"),
  legend.position = "bottom",
  legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
  legend.text  = element_text(size = 9)
)

# Non-centered maps (viridis option "C")
p_honor <- ggplot(filter(world_long, Dimension == "Honor")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "D",
                     limits = lims, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Honor norms") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

p_face <- ggplot(filter(world_long, Dimension == "Face")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "D",
                     limits = lims, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Face norms") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

p_dignity <- ggplot(filter(world_long, Dimension == "Dignity")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "D",
                     limits = lims, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Dignity norms") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

# Centered maps (viridis option "D" to visually differentiate from non-centered)
p_honorc <- ggplot(filter(world_long_centered, Dimension == "HonorC")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "C",
                     limits = limsc, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Honor norms (centred)") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

p_facec <- ggplot(filter(world_long_centered, Dimension == "FaceC")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "C",
                     limits = limsc, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Face norms (centred)") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

p_dignityc <- ggplot(filter(world_long_centered, Dimension == "DignityC")) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Score), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis(name = "Societal mean (1-7)", option = "C",
                     limits = limsc, na.value = "grey92", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Dignity norms (centred)") +
  theme_void() +
  common_theme

# ---- build two separate rows (each with its own collected legend) ----
# Keep guide collection INSIDE each row so the top row (C) and bottom row (D)
# retain different palettes and their own legends.
row_nc <- (p_honor | p_face | p_dignity) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",          # put legend to the side of the row
    legend.direction = "vertical"       # vertical legend for compact height
  )

row_c <- (p_honorc | p_facec | p_dignityc) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.direction = "vertical"
  )

# Final assembly: stack the two rows. Each keeps its own legend and color scale.
final_plot <- row_nc / row_c
final_plot

# Export plot
ggsave("figures/DHF_Norms_worldmap.png", final_plot,
       width = 18, height = 6, dpi = 600)
ggsave("figures/DHF_Norms_worldmap.pdf", final_plot,
       width = 18, height = 6)

Main study: Country-level correlates of DHF

Ecological antecedents of DHF endorsement and norms

apaTables::apa.cor.table(
  dt4 %>% 
    select(D_normc_mean_country, H_normc_mean_country,
           F_normc_mean_country, D_endc_mean_country, 
           H_endc_mean_country, F_endc_mean_country, 
           wb_pop_density_1961, ms_pathogen_9items, 
           icb_crisis_participations, icb_avg_violence_level,
           wb_gdp_pcap_usd_1960, fiw_F_2013, 
           fiw_C_2013, fiw_Total_2013
           )
)

DHF and other cultural dimensions

# believe these are mean_country centered above, but should check
apaTables::apa.cor.table(
  dt4 %>% 
    select(D_normc_mean_country, H_normc_mean_country,
           F_normc_mean_country, D_endc_mean_country, 
           H_endc_mean_country, F_endc_mean_country, 
           # Openness_to_Change_mean_country,
           Openness_to_Change, 
           # Conservation_mean_country, 
           Conservation, 
           # Self_Transcendence_mean_country,
           Self_Transcendence, 
           # Self_Enhancement_mean_country, 
           Self_Enhancement, 
           tl_tightness, mk_individualism_collectivism, 
           mk_flexibility_monumentalism, rm_relational_mobility 
           # `o-c`, `st-se` 
           # not sure what these are need to update var names
           )
)

## The confidence interval is a plausible range of population correlations
