Main study: Individual-level correlates of DHF

DHF and prosociality

[Tables in line with prereg H2-H3]

# ============================================================================
# H2 ANALYSIS USING dt5 (PROSOCIAL OUTCOME)
# ============================================================================

# reset dt5 so that we have all the variables 
dt5 <- subset(dt4, dictator_cpass==1) 
dt5$prosocial <- as.numeric(dt5$Dictator_decision)

print("Preparing H2 analysis with misty centering")
## [1] "Preparing H2 analysis with misty centering"
print(paste("Dataset has", nrow(dt5), "participants"))
## [1] "Dataset has 21515 participants"
# Proper multilevel centering using misty package
DF_H2 <- dt5 %>% 
  mutate(
    # Group-mean centering for individual-level variables (Level 1)
    D_self_end_CWC = center(D_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    D_other_end_CWC = center(D_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full), 
    H_self_end_CWC = center(H_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_other_end_CWC = center(H_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full), 
    F_self_end_CWC = center(F_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_other_end_CWC = center(F_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full)
  )

# Data quality check
print("Data quality check...")
## [1] "Data quality check..."
analysis_vars <- c("prosocial", "D_self_end_CWC", "D_other_end_CWC", 
                   "H_self_end_CWC", "H_other_end_CWC", 
                   "F_self_end_CWC", "F_other_end_CWC", "country_res_full")

# Check which variables exist
available_vars <- analysis_vars[analysis_vars %in% names(DF_H2)]
print("Available analysis variables:")
## [1] "Available analysis variables:"
print(available_vars)
## [1] "prosocial"        "D_self_end_CWC"   "D_other_end_CWC"  "H_self_end_CWC"  
## [5] "H_other_end_CWC"  "F_self_end_CWC"   "F_other_end_CWC"  "country_res_full"
# Complete cases
complete_cases <- sum(complete.cases(DF_H2[, available_vars]))
print(paste("Complete cases for analysis:", complete_cases, "out of", nrow(DF_H2)))
## [1] "Complete cases for analysis: 21510 out of 21515"
# Country sample sizes
country_sizes <- DF_H2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(prosocial)) %>%
  group_by(country_res_full) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

print("Sample sizes by country (top 10):")
## [1] "Sample sizes by country (top 10):"
print(head(country_sizes, 10))
## # A tibble: 10 × 2
##    country_res_full     n
##    <fct>            <int>
##  1 Turkey            1772
##  2 United States     1426
##  3 Australia          847
##  4 Philippines        752
##  5 Germany            678
##  6 Canada             648
##  7 Croatia            637
##  8 Poland             635
##  9 Italy              544
## 10 United Kingdom     465
print(paste("Number of countries:", nrow(country_sizes)))
## [1] "Number of countries: 63"
print(paste("Minimum country sample size:", min(country_sizes$n)))
## [1] "Minimum country sample size: 49"
# H2 Main Analysis - six factors model 
print("\nRunning H2 Analysis - Six DHF Facets Model...")
## [1] "\nRunning H2 Analysis - Six DHF Facets Model..."
h2.fit <- lmer(prosocial ~ D_self_end_CWC + D_other_end_CWC + 
                 H_self_end_CWC + H_other_end_CWC + 
                 F_self_end_CWC + F_other_end_CWC +
                 (1|country_res_full), 
               data = DF_H2,
               REML = FALSE)

print("H2 MODEL RESULTS:")
## [1] "H2 MODEL RESULTS:"
h2_params <- parameters(h2.fit, standardize = "refit", ci = 0.95)
print(h2_params)
## # Fixed Effects
## 
## Parameter       | Coefficient |       SE |         95% CI | t(21501) |      p
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
## (Intercept)     |        0.01 |     0.02 | [-0.03,  0.06] |     0.62 | 0.537 
## D self end CWC  |   -4.15e-03 | 7.56e-03 | [-0.02,  0.01] |    -0.55 | 0.583 
## D other end CWC |        0.08 | 7.56e-03 | [ 0.07,  0.10] |    10.88 | < .001
## H self end CWC  |       -0.03 | 8.14e-03 | [-0.04, -0.01] |    -3.11 | 0.002 
## H other end CWC |       -0.01 | 9.19e-03 | [-0.03,  0.01] |    -1.14 | 0.255 
## F self end CWC  |       -0.03 | 9.07e-03 | [-0.04, -0.01] |    -2.84 | 0.005 
## F other end CWC |        0.04 | 8.87e-03 | [ 0.03,  0.06] |     4.92 | < .001
## 
## # Random Effects
## 
## Parameter                        | Coefficient
## ----------------------------------------------
## SD (Intercept: country_res_full) |        0.18
## SD (Residual)                    |        0.98
## 
## Uncertainty intervals (equal-tailed) and p-values (two-tailed) computed
##   using a Wald t-distribution approximation. Uncertainty intervals for
##   random effect variances computed using a Wald z-distribution
##   approximation.
# Model performance
print("\nModel Performance:")
## [1] "\nModel Performance:"
perf <- performance(h2.fit)
## Model was not fitted with REML, however, `estimator = "REML"`. Set
##   `estimator = "ML"` to obtain identical results as from `AIC()`.
print(perf)
## # Indices of model performance
## 
## AIC     |    AICc |     BIC | R2 (cond.) | R2 (marg.) |   ICC |  RMSE | Sigma
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 84649.9 | 84649.9 | 84721.7 |      0.041 |      0.008 | 0.032 | 1.721 | 1.723
# R-squared values
r2_vals <- r2(h2.fit)
print(paste("Marginal R² (fixed effects):", round(r2_vals$R2_marginal, 3)))
## [1] "Marginal R² (fixed effects): 0.008"
print(paste("Conditional R² (fixed + random):", round(r2_vals$R2_conditional, 3)))
## [1] "Conditional R² (fixed + random): 0.041"
# Check column names and create standardized results table
print("\nColumn names in h2_params:")
## [1] "\nColumn names in h2_params:"
print(names(h2_params))
##  [1] "Parameter"   "Coefficient" "SE"          "CI"          "CI_low"     
##  [6] "CI_high"     "t"           "df_error"    "p"           "Effects"    
## [11] "Group"
# Create standardized results table with proper column references
print("\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI and p-values:")
## [1] "\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI and p-values:"
h2_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = h2_params$Parameter,
  Std_Beta = round(h2_params$Coefficient, 4),  # Using Coefficient instead of Std_Coefficient
  CI_Lower = round(h2_params$CI_low, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(h2_params$CI_high, 4),
  p_value = round(h2_params$p, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(h2_params$p < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(h2_params$p < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(h2_params$p < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(h2_results)
##           Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1       (Intercept)   0.0150  -0.0326   0.0625  0.5374             
## 2    D_self_end_CWC  -0.0042  -0.0190   0.0107  0.5832             
## 3   D_other_end_CWC   0.0822   0.0674   0.0971  0.0000          ***
## 4    H_self_end_CWC  -0.0254  -0.0413  -0.0094  0.0019           **
## 5   H_other_end_CWC  -0.0105  -0.0285   0.0075  0.2545             
## 6    F_self_end_CWC  -0.0258  -0.0435  -0.0080  0.0045           **
## 7   F_other_end_CWC   0.0437   0.0263   0.0611  0.0000          ***
## 8    SD (Intercept)   0.1802       NA       NA      NA         <NA>
## 9 SD (Observations)   0.9847       NA       NA      NA         <NA>
# Random effects
rand_effects <- VarCorr(h2.fit)
print("\nRandom Effects Variance:")
## [1] "\nRandom Effects Variance:"
print(rand_effects)
##  Groups           Name        Std.Dev.
##  country_res_full (Intercept) 0.31529 
##  Residual                     1.72287
# ============================================================================
# H3 ANALYSIS - MULTILEVEL PATH MODELS
# ===========================================================================

# STEP 1: Prepare prosocial data (dt5) with proper misty centering
print("Preparing prosocial model data from dt5...")
## [1] "Preparing prosocial model data from dt5..."
DF_prosocial_H3 <- dt5 %>%
  filter(!is.na(prosocial) & !is.na(D_end) & !is.na(H_end) & !is.na(F_end) &
           !is.na(D_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(H_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(F_norm_mean_country)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Individual-level variables: group-mean centering (CWC)
    D_end_CWC = center(D_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_end_CWC = center(H_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_end_CWC = center(F_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    
    # Country-level variables: grand-mean centering (CGM)
    D_norm_agg_CGM = center(D_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    H_norm_agg_CGM = center(H_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    F_norm_agg_CGM = center(F_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM")
  )

print(paste("Prosocial model sample size:", nrow(DF_prosocial_H3)))
## [1] "Prosocial model sample size: 21510"
# STEP 2: Prepare reciprocity data (dt6) with proper misty centering
# reset dt6 since it doesn't have these variables 
dt6 <- subset(dt4, ultimatum_cpass==1) #N=20626

# Merge with dt6
dt6 <- merge(dt6, neg_rec_df, by="ResponseId")
dt6$UG_negrec <- as.numeric(dt6$UG_negrec)
dt6 <- merge(dt6, pos_rec_df, by="ResponseId")

print("Preparing reciprocity model data from dt6...")
## [1] "Preparing reciprocity model data from dt6..."
DF_reciprocity_H3 <- dt6 %>%
  filter(!is.na(UG_posrec) & !is.na(UG_negrec) & 
           !is.na(D_end) & !is.na(H_end) & !is.na(F_end) &
           !is.na(D_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(H_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(F_norm_mean_country)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Individual-level variables: group-mean centering (CWC)
    D_end_CWC = center(D_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_end_CWC = center(H_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_end_CWC = center(F_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    
    # Country-level variables: grand-mean centering (CGM) 
    D_norm_agg_CGM = center(D_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    H_norm_agg_CGM = center(H_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    F_norm_agg_CGM = center(F_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM")
  )

print(paste("Reciprocity model sample size:", nrow(DF_reciprocity_H3)))
## [1] "Reciprocity model sample size: 20494"
# STEP 3: H3 Model 1 - Prosocial Behavior
print("\nRunning H3 Model 1: Prosocial Behavior...")
## [1] "\nRunning H3 Model 1: Prosocial Behavior..."
prosocial.model <- '
# Level 1: Individual endorsement predicting prosocial behavior
prosocial ~ D_end_CWC + H_end_CWC + F_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting prosocial behavior  
prosocial ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

prosocial.fit <- sem(
  model = prosocial.model,
  data = DF_prosocial_H3,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("PROSOCIAL MODEL RESULTS:")
## [1] "PROSOCIAL MODEL RESULTS:"
print(summary(prosocial.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                         8
## 
##   Number of observations                         21510
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled
##   Test Statistic                                 0.000       0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0           0
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                               203.462      41.153
##   Degrees of freedom                                 6           6
##   P-value                                        0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  4.944
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    1.000       1.000
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       1.000       1.000
##                                                                   
##   Robust Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                            NA
##   Robust Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                               NA
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)             -42451.059  -42451.059
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)     -42451.059  -42451.059
##                                                                   
##   Akaike (AIC)                               84918.118   84918.118
##   Bayesian (BIC)                             84981.928   84981.928
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)      84956.505   84956.505
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.000          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                       NA          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                       NA          NA
##                                                                   
##   Robust RMSEA                                               0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower                     0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper                     0.000
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA <= 0.050                            NA
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA >= 0.080                            NA
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.000       0.000
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                       Robust.cluster
##   Information                                 Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Regressions:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   prosocial ~                                                           
##     D_end_CWC         0.206    0.036    5.748    0.000    0.206    0.068
##     H_end_CWC        -0.109    0.022   -4.973    0.000   -0.109   -0.043
##     F_end_CWC         0.064    0.021    2.988    0.003    0.064    0.026
##     D_norm_agg_CGM    0.383    0.107    3.586    0.000    0.383    0.076
##     H_norm_agg_CGM    0.694    0.200    3.479    0.001    0.694    0.083
##     F_norm_agg_CGM   -0.430    0.193   -2.228    0.026   -0.430   -0.065
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .prosocial         4.767    0.033  146.340    0.000    4.767    2.725
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .prosocial         3.032    0.143   21.202    0.000    3.032    0.991
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     prosocial         0.009
# Extract standardized results for prosocial model
prosocial_std <- standardizedSolution(prosocial.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
prosocial_reg <- prosocial_std[prosocial_std$op == "~",]
print("\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
prosocial_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = prosocial_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(prosocial_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(prosocial_reg$ci.lower, 4), 
  CI_Upper = round(prosocial_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(prosocial_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(prosocial_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(prosocial_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(prosocial_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(prosocial_results)
##        Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1      D_end_CWC   0.0680   0.0446   0.0914  0.0000          ***
## 2      H_end_CWC  -0.0434  -0.0610  -0.0257  0.0000          ***
## 3      F_end_CWC   0.0257   0.0090   0.0424  0.0026           **
## 4 D_norm_agg_CGM   0.0757   0.0350   0.1165  0.0003          ***
## 5 H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0832   0.0362   0.1302  0.0005          ***
## 6 F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0646  -0.1217  -0.0075  0.0265            *
# STEP 4: H3 Model 2 - Positive Reciprocity
print("\nRunning H3 Model 2: Positive Reciprocity...")
## [1] "\nRunning H3 Model 2: Positive Reciprocity..."
posrec.model <- '
# Level 1: Individual endorsement predicting positive reciprocity
UG_posrec ~ D_end_CWC + H_end_CWC + F_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting positive reciprocity
UG_posrec ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

posrec.fit <- sem(
  model = posrec.model,
  data = DF_reciprocity_H3,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("POSITIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL RESULTS:")
## [1] "POSITIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL RESULTS:"
print(summary(posrec.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                         8
## 
##   Number of observations                         20494
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled
##   Test Statistic                                 0.000       0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0           0
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                               132.507      43.764
##   Degrees of freedom                                 6           6
##   P-value                                        0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  3.028
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    1.000       1.000
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       1.000       1.000
##                                                                   
##   Robust Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                            NA
##   Robust Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                               NA
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)             -96148.591  -96148.591
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)     -96148.591  -96148.591
##                                                                   
##   Akaike (AIC)                              192313.183  192313.183
##   Bayesian (BIC)                            192376.606  192376.606
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)     192351.182  192351.182
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.000          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                       NA          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                       NA          NA
##                                                                   
##   Robust RMSEA                                               0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower                     0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper                     0.000
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA <= 0.050                            NA
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA >= 0.080                            NA
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.000       0.000
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                       Robust.cluster
##   Information                                 Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Regressions:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   UG_posrec ~                                                           
##     D_end_CWC         3.036    0.326    9.313    0.000    3.036    0.066
##     H_end_CWC        -1.621    0.317   -5.119    0.000   -1.621   -0.042
##     F_end_CWC         1.701    0.400    4.248    0.000    1.701    0.045
##     D_norm_agg_CGM   -0.700    1.549   -0.452    0.651   -0.700   -0.009
##     H_norm_agg_CGM    0.403    2.788    0.144    0.885    0.403    0.003
##     F_norm_agg_CGM    2.680    2.300    1.165    0.244    2.680    0.027
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .UG_posrec        74.226    0.376  197.422    0.000   74.226    2.805
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .UG_posrec       695.909    8.495   81.922    0.000  695.909    0.994
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     UG_posrec         0.006
# Extract standardized results for positive reciprocity model
posrec_std <- standardizedSolution(posrec.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
posrec_reg <- posrec_std[posrec_std$op == "~",]
print("\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
posrec_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = posrec_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(posrec_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(posrec_reg$ci.lower, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(posrec_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(posrec_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(posrec_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(posrec_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(posrec_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(posrec_results)
##        Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1      D_end_CWC   0.0656   0.0518   0.0794  0.0000          ***
## 2      H_end_CWC  -0.0423  -0.0585  -0.0261  0.0000          ***
## 3      F_end_CWC   0.0450   0.0242   0.0659  0.0000          ***
## 4 D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0092  -0.0490   0.0306  0.6512             
## 5 H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0032  -0.0405   0.0469  0.8852             
## 6 F_norm_agg_CGM   0.0266  -0.0181   0.0712  0.2439
# STEP 5: H3 Model 3 - Negative Reciprocity  
print("\nRunning H3 Model 3: Negative Reciprocity...")
## [1] "\nRunning H3 Model 3: Negative Reciprocity..."
negrec.model <- '
# Level 1: Individual endorsement predicting negative reciprocity
UG_negrec ~ D_end_CWC + H_end_CWC + F_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting negative reciprocity
UG_negrec ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

negrec.fit <- sem(
  model = negrec.model,
  data = DF_reciprocity_H3,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL RESULTS:")
## [1] "NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL RESULTS:"
print(summary(negrec.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                         8
## 
##   Number of observations                         20494
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled
##   Test Statistic                                 0.000       0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0           0
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                               235.614      28.716
##   Degrees of freedom                                 6           6
##   P-value                                        0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  8.205
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    1.000       1.000
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       1.000       1.000

##   Robust Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                               NA
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)             -44116.520  -44116.520
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)     -44116.520  -44116.520
##                                                                   
##   Akaike (AIC)                               88249.041   88249.041
##   Bayesian (BIC)                             88312.464   88312.464
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)      88287.040   88287.040
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.000          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                       NA          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                       NA          NA
##                                                                   
##   Robust RMSEA                                               0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower                     0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper                     0.000
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA <= 0.050                            NA
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA >= 0.080                            NA
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.000       0.000
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                       Robust.cluster
##   Information                                 Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Regressions:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   UG_negrec ~                                                           
##     D_end_CWC        -0.061    0.039   -1.579    0.114   -0.061   -0.017
##     H_end_CWC         0.291    0.034    8.574    0.000    0.291    0.096
##     F_end_CWC        -0.122    0.031   -3.965    0.000   -0.122   -0.041
##     D_norm_agg_CGM   -0.378    0.173   -2.188    0.029   -0.378   -0.063
##     H_norm_agg_CGM    0.247    0.383    0.643    0.520    0.247    0.025
##     F_norm_agg_CGM   -0.042    0.174   -0.241    0.810   -0.042   -0.005
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .UG_negrec         4.435    0.059   75.273    0.000    4.435    2.117
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .UG_negrec         4.338    0.133   32.733    0.000    4.338    0.989
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     UG_negrec         0.011
# Extract standardized results for negative reciprocity model
negrec_std <- standardizedSolution(negrec.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
negrec_reg <- negrec_std[negrec_std$op == "~",]
print("\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
negrec_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = negrec_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(negrec_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(negrec_reg$ci.lower, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(negrec_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(negrec_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(negrec_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(negrec_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(negrec_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(negrec_results)
##        Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1      D_end_CWC  -0.0166  -0.0372   0.0040  0.1137             
## 2      H_end_CWC   0.0960   0.0739   0.1181  0.0000          ***
## 3      F_end_CWC  -0.0408  -0.0608  -0.0208  0.0001          ***
## 4 D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0626  -0.1186  -0.0066  0.0285            *
## 5 H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0249  -0.0511   0.1009  0.5208             
## 6 F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0052  -0.0479   0.0374  0.8098
# R-squared comparison
print("\nR-SQUARED COMPARISON:")
## [1] "\nR-SQUARED COMPARISON:"
cat("Prosocial Behavior: R² = ", round(inspect(prosocial.fit, "r2"), 3), "\n")
## Prosocial Behavior: R² =  0.009
cat("Positive Reciprocity: R² = ", round(inspect(posrec.fit, "r2"), 3), "\n") 
## Positive Reciprocity: R² =  0.006
cat("Negative Reciprocity: R² = ", round(inspect(negrec.fit, "r2"), 3), "\n")
## Negative Reciprocity: R² =  0.011
print("\nH2 MODEL - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nH2 MODEL - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
print(h2_results)
##           Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1       (Intercept)   0.0150  -0.0326   0.0625  0.5374             
## 2    D_self_end_CWC  -0.0042  -0.0190   0.0107  0.5832             
## 3   D_other_end_CWC   0.0822   0.0674   0.0971  0.0000          ***
## 4    H_self_end_CWC  -0.0254  -0.0413  -0.0094  0.0019           **
## 5   H_other_end_CWC  -0.0105  -0.0285   0.0075  0.2545             
## 6    F_self_end_CWC  -0.0258  -0.0435  -0.0080  0.0045           **
## 7   F_other_end_CWC   0.0437   0.0263   0.0611  0.0000          ***
## 8    SD (Intercept)   0.1802       NA       NA      NA         <NA>
## 9 SD (Observations)   0.9847       NA       NA      NA         <NA>
print("\nPROSOCIAL MODEL - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nPROSOCIAL MODEL - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
print(prosocial_results)
##        Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1      D_end_CWC   0.0680   0.0446   0.0914  0.0000          ***
## 2      H_end_CWC  -0.0434  -0.0610  -0.0257  0.0000          ***
## 3      F_end_CWC   0.0257   0.0090   0.0424  0.0026           **
## 4 D_norm_agg_CGM   0.0757   0.0350   0.1165  0.0003          ***
## 5 H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0832   0.0362   0.1302  0.0005          ***
## 6 F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0646  -0.1217  -0.0075  0.0265            *
print("\nPOSITIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nPOSITIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
print(posrec_results)
##        Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1      D_end_CWC   0.0656   0.0518   0.0794  0.0000          ***
## 2      H_end_CWC  -0.0423  -0.0585  -0.0261  0.0000          ***
## 3      F_end_CWC   0.0450   0.0242   0.0659  0.0000          ***
## 4 D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0092  -0.0490   0.0306  0.6512             
## 5 H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0032  -0.0405   0.0469  0.8852             
## 6 F_norm_agg_CGM   0.0266  -0.0181   0.0712  0.2439
print("\nNEGATIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nNEGATIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
print(negrec_results)
##        Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1      D_end_CWC  -0.0166  -0.0372   0.0040  0.1137             
## 2      H_end_CWC   0.0960   0.0739   0.1181  0.0000          ***
## 3      F_end_CWC  -0.0408  -0.0608  -0.0208  0.0001          ***
## 4 D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0626  -0.1186  -0.0066  0.0285            *
## 5 H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0249  -0.0511   0.1009  0.5208             
## 6 F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0052  -0.0479   0.0374  0.8098
# ============================================================================
# H3 ANALYSIS - MULTILEVEL PATH MODELS WITH 6-FACTOR INDIVIDUAL PREDICTORS
# ============================================================================

print("Preparing H3 multilevel path model analysis with 6-factor individual predictors...")
## [1] "Preparing H3 multilevel path model analysis with 6-factor individual predictors..."
print("Using dt5 for prosocial, dt6 for reciprocity outcomes...")
## [1] "Using dt5 for prosocial, dt6 for reciprocity outcomes..."
# STEP 1: Prepare prosocial data (dt5) with 6-factor individual predictors + country norms
print("Preparing prosocial model data from dt5...")
## [1] "Preparing prosocial model data from dt5..."
DF_prosocial_H3_6factor <- dt5 %>%
  filter(!is.na(prosocial) & 
           !is.na(D_self_end) & !is.na(D_other_end) & 
           !is.na(H_self_end) & !is.na(H_other_end) & 
           !is.na(F_self_end) & !is.na(F_other_end) &
           !is.na(D_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(H_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(F_norm_mean_country)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Individual-level variables: 6-factor model with group-mean centering (CWC)
    D_self_end_CWC = center(D_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    D_other_end_CWC = center(D_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_self_end_CWC = center(H_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_other_end_CWC = center(H_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_self_end_CWC = center(F_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_other_end_CWC = center(F_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    
    # Country-level variables: grand-mean centering (CGM)
    D_norm_agg_CGM = center(D_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    H_norm_agg_CGM = center(H_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    F_norm_agg_CGM = center(F_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM")
  )

print(paste("Prosocial model sample size:", nrow(DF_prosocial_H3_6factor)))
## [1] "Prosocial model sample size: 21510"
# STEP 2: Prepare reciprocity data (dt6) with 6-factor individual predictors + country norms
print("Preparing reciprocity model data from dt6...")
## [1] "Preparing reciprocity model data from dt6..."
DF_reciprocity_H3_6factor <- dt6 %>%
  filter(!is.na(UG_posrec) & !is.na(UG_negrec) & 
           !is.na(D_self_end) & !is.na(D_other_end) & 
           !is.na(H_self_end) & !is.na(H_other_end) & 
           !is.na(F_self_end) & !is.na(F_other_end) &
           !is.na(D_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(H_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(F_norm_mean_country)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Individual-level variables: 6-factor model with group-mean centering (CWC)
    D_self_end_CWC = center(D_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    D_other_end_CWC = center(D_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_self_end_CWC = center(H_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_other_end_CWC = center(H_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_self_end_CWC = center(F_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_other_end_CWC = center(F_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    
    # Country-level variables: grand-mean centering (CGM) 
    D_norm_agg_CGM = center(D_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    H_norm_agg_CGM = center(H_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    F_norm_agg_CGM = center(F_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM")
  )

print(paste("Reciprocity model sample size:", nrow(DF_reciprocity_H3_6factor)))
## [1] "Reciprocity model sample size: 20494"
# STEP 3: H3 Model 1 - Prosocial Behavior (6-factor individual predictors)
print("\nRunning H3 Model 1: Prosocial Behavior with 6-Factor Individual Predictors...")
## [1] "\nRunning H3 Model 1: Prosocial Behavior with 6-Factor Individual Predictors..."
prosocial_6factor.model <- '
# Level 1: 6-factor individual endorsement predicting prosocial behavior
prosocial ~ D_self_end_CWC + D_other_end_CWC + 
            H_self_end_CWC + H_other_end_CWC + 
            F_self_end_CWC + F_other_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting prosocial behavior  
prosocial ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

prosocial_6factor.fit <- sem(
  model = prosocial_6factor.model,
  data = DF_prosocial_H3_6factor,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("PROSOCIAL MODEL RESULTS (6-Factor Individual Predictors):")
## [1] "PROSOCIAL MODEL RESULTS (6-Factor Individual Predictors):"
print(summary(prosocial_6factor.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                        11
## 
##   Number of observations                         21510
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled
##   Test Statistic                                 0.000       0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0           0
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                               274.973      74.437
##   Degrees of freedom                                 9           9
##   P-value                                        0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  3.694
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    1.000       1.000
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       1.000       1.000
##                                                                   
##   Robust Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                            NA
##   Robust Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                               NA
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)             -42415.303  -42415.303
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)     -42415.303  -42415.303
##                                                                   
##   Akaike (AIC)                               84852.607   84852.607
##   Bayesian (BIC)                             84940.346   84940.346
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)      84905.388   84905.388
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.000          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                       NA          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                       NA          NA
##                                                                   
##   Robust RMSEA                                               0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower                     0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper                     0.000
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA <= 0.050                            NA
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA >= 0.080                            NA
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.000       0.000
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                       Robust.cluster
##   Information                                 Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Regressions:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   prosocial ~                                                           
##     D_self_end_CWC   -0.011    0.026   -0.433    0.665   -0.011   -0.004
##     D_other_nd_CWC    0.200    0.021    9.305    0.000    0.200    0.082
##     H_self_end_CWC   -0.057    0.023   -2.486    0.013   -0.057   -0.025
##     H_other_nd_CWC   -0.022    0.022   -1.019    0.308   -0.022   -0.010
##     F_self_end_CWC   -0.059    0.024   -2.492    0.013   -0.059   -0.026
##     F_other_nd_CWC    0.093    0.019    4.947    0.000    0.093    0.044
##     D_norm_agg_CGM    0.383    0.107    3.586    0.000    0.383    0.076
##     H_norm_agg_CGM    0.694    0.200    3.479    0.001    0.694    0.083
##     F_norm_agg_CGM   -0.430    0.193   -2.228    0.026   -0.430   -0.065
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .prosocial         4.767    0.033  146.340    0.000    4.767    2.725
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .prosocial         3.022    0.143   21.188    0.000    3.022    0.987
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     prosocial         0.013
# Extract standardized results for prosocial model
prosocial_6factor_std <- standardizedSolution(prosocial_6factor.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
prosocial_6factor_reg <- prosocial_6factor_std[prosocial_6factor_std$op == "~",]
print("\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
prosocial_6factor_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = prosocial_6factor_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(prosocial_6factor_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(prosocial_6factor_reg$ci.lower, 4), 
  CI_Upper = round(prosocial_6factor_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(prosocial_6factor_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(prosocial_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(prosocial_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(prosocial_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(prosocial_6factor_results)
##         Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1  D_self_end_CWC  -0.0042  -0.0229   0.0146  0.6650             
## 2 D_other_end_CWC   0.0822   0.0648   0.0997  0.0000          ***
## 3  H_self_end_CWC  -0.0254  -0.0457  -0.0050  0.0147            *
## 4 H_other_end_CWC  -0.0105  -0.0306   0.0096  0.3077             
## 5  F_self_end_CWC  -0.0258  -0.0459  -0.0056  0.0122            *
## 6 F_other_end_CWC   0.0437   0.0269   0.0605  0.0000          ***
## 7  D_norm_agg_CGM   0.0757   0.0350   0.1165  0.0003          ***
## 8  H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0832   0.0362   0.1302  0.0005          ***
## 9  F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0646  -0.1217  -0.0075  0.0265            *
# STEP 4: H3 Model 2 - Positive Reciprocity (6-factor individual predictors)
print("\nRunning H3 Model 2: Positive Reciprocity with 6-Factor Individual Predictors...")
## [1] "\nRunning H3 Model 2: Positive Reciprocity with 6-Factor Individual Predictors..."
posrec_6factor.model <- '
# Level 1: 6-factor individual endorsement predicting positive reciprocity
UG_posrec ~ D_self_end_CWC + D_other_end_CWC + 
            H_self_end_CWC + H_other_end_CWC + 
            F_self_end_CWC + F_other_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting positive reciprocity
UG_posrec ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

posrec_6factor.fit <- sem(
  model = posrec_6factor.model,
  data = DF_reciprocity_H3_6factor,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("POSITIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL RESULTS (6-Factor Individual Predictors):")
## [1] "POSITIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL RESULTS (6-Factor Individual Predictors):"
print(summary(posrec_6factor.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                        11
## 
##   Number of observations                         20494
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled
##   Test Statistic                                 0.000       0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0           0
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                               158.274      63.226
##   Degrees of freedom                                 9           9
##   P-value                                        0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  2.503
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    1.000       1.000
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       1.000       1.000
##                                                                   
##   Robust Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                            NA
##   Robust Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                               NA
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)             -96135.708  -96135.708
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)     -96135.708  -96135.708

##   Akaike (AIC)                              192293.416  192293.416
##   Bayesian (BIC)                            192380.623  192380.623
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)     192345.665  192345.665
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.000          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                       NA          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                       NA          NA
##                                                                   
##   Robust RMSEA                                               0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower                     0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper                     0.000
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA <= 0.050                            NA
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA >= 0.080                            NA
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.000       0.000

## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                       Robust.cluster
##   Information                                 Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Regressions:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   UG_posrec ~                                                           
##     D_self_end_CWC    0.371    0.337    1.100    0.271    0.371    0.009
##     D_other_nd_CWC    2.430    0.332    7.326    0.000    2.430    0.066
##     H_self_end_CWC   -1.189    0.330   -3.602    0.000   -1.189   -0.035
##     H_other_nd_CWC   -0.133    0.343   -0.387    0.699   -0.133   -0.004
##     F_self_end_CWC    0.456    0.413    1.103    0.270    0.456    0.013
##     F_other_nd_CWC    0.929    0.310    2.998    0.003    0.929    0.029
##     D_norm_agg_CGM   -0.700    1.549   -0.452    0.651   -0.700   -0.009
##     H_norm_agg_CGM    0.403    2.788    0.144    0.885    0.403    0.003
##     F_norm_agg_CGM    2.680    2.300    1.165    0.244    2.680    0.027
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .UG_posrec        74.226    0.376  197.422    0.000   74.226    2.805
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .UG_posrec       695.035    8.356   83.182    0.000  695.035    0.992
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     UG_posrec         0.008
# Extract standardized results for positive reciprocity model
posrec_6factor_std <- standardizedSolution(posrec_6factor.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
posrec_6factor_reg <- posrec_6factor_std[posrec_6factor_std$op == "~",]
print("\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
posrec_6factor_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = posrec_6factor_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(posrec_6factor_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(posrec_6factor_reg$ci.lower, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(posrec_6factor_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(posrec_6factor_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(posrec_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(posrec_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(posrec_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(posrec_6factor_results)
##         Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1  D_self_end_CWC   0.0091  -0.0071   0.0253  0.2721             
## 2 D_other_end_CWC   0.0657   0.0483   0.0830  0.0000          ***
## 3  H_self_end_CWC  -0.0348  -0.0538  -0.0159  0.0003          ***
## 4 H_other_end_CWC  -0.0041  -0.0251   0.0168  0.6986             
## 5  F_self_end_CWC   0.0131  -0.0102   0.0365  0.2706             
## 6 F_other_end_CWC   0.0286   0.0099   0.0472  0.0027           **
## 7  D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0092  -0.0490   0.0306  0.6512             
## 8  H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0032  -0.0405   0.0469  0.8852             
## 9  F_norm_agg_CGM   0.0266  -0.0181   0.0712  0.2439
# STEP 5: H3 Model 3 - Negative Reciprocity (6-factor individual predictors)
print("\nRunning H3 Model 3: Negative Reciprocity with 6-Factor Individual Predictors...")
## [1] "\nRunning H3 Model 3: Negative Reciprocity with 6-Factor Individual Predictors..."
negrec_6factor.model <- '
# Level 1: 6-factor individual endorsement predicting negative reciprocity
UG_negrec ~ D_self_end_CWC + D_other_end_CWC + 
            H_self_end_CWC + H_other_end_CWC + 
            F_self_end_CWC + F_other_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting negative reciprocity
UG_negrec ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

negrec_6factor.fit <- sem(
  model = negrec_6factor.model,
  data = DF_reciprocity_H3_6factor,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL RESULTS (6-Factor Individual Predictors):")
## [1] "NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL RESULTS (6-Factor Individual Predictors):"
print(summary(negrec_6factor.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                        11
## 
##   Number of observations                         20494
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled
##   Test Statistic                                 0.000       0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0           0
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                               292.611      48.155
##   Degrees of freedom                                 9           9
##   P-value                                        0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  6.076
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    1.000       1.000
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       1.000       1.000
##                                                                   
##   Robust Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                            NA
##   Robust Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                               NA
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)             -44088.022  -44088.022
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)     -44088.022  -44088.022
##                                                                   
##   Akaike (AIC)                               88198.044   88198.044
##   Bayesian (BIC)                             88285.250   88285.250
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)      88250.293   88250.293
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.000          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                       NA          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                       NA          NA
##                                                                   
##   Robust RMSEA                                               0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower                     0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper                     0.000
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA <= 0.050                            NA
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA >= 0.080                            NA
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.000       0.000
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                       Robust.cluster
##   Information                                 Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Regressions:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   UG_negrec ~                                                           
##     D_self_end_CWC    0.033    0.030    1.093    0.274    0.033    0.010
##     D_other_nd_CWC   -0.075    0.030   -2.475    0.013   -0.075   -0.026
##     H_self_end_CWC    0.215    0.034    6.348    0.000    0.215    0.080
##     H_other_nd_CWC    0.046    0.025    1.830    0.067    0.046    0.018
##     F_self_end_CWC    0.057    0.033    1.728    0.084    0.057    0.021
##     F_other_nd_CWC   -0.134    0.033   -4.049    0.000   -0.134   -0.052
##     D_norm_agg_CGM   -0.378    0.173   -2.188    0.029   -0.378   -0.063
##     H_norm_agg_CGM    0.247    0.383    0.643    0.520    0.247    0.025
##     F_norm_agg_CGM   -0.042    0.174   -0.241    0.810   -0.042   -0.005
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .UG_negrec         4.435    0.059   75.273    0.000    4.435    2.117
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .UG_negrec         4.326    0.132   32.735    0.000    4.326    0.986
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     UG_negrec         0.014
# Extract standardized results for negative reciprocity model
negrec_6factor_std <- standardizedSolution(negrec_6factor.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
negrec_6factor_reg <- negrec_6factor_std[negrec_6factor_std$op == "~",]
print("\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nStandardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
negrec_6factor_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = negrec_6factor_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(negrec_6factor_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(negrec_6factor_reg$ci.lower, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(negrec_6factor_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(negrec_6factor_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(negrec_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(negrec_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(negrec_6factor_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(negrec_6factor_results)
##         Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1  D_self_end_CWC   0.0102  -0.0081   0.0285  0.2747             
## 2 D_other_end_CWC  -0.0256  -0.0458  -0.0054  0.0131            *
## 3  H_self_end_CWC   0.0795   0.0551   0.1040  0.0000          ***
## 4 H_other_end_CWC   0.0180  -0.0014   0.0374  0.0688             
## 5  F_self_end_CWC   0.0206  -0.0029   0.0441  0.0851             
## 6 F_other_end_CWC  -0.0522  -0.0775  -0.0268  0.0001          ***
## 7  D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0626  -0.1186  -0.0066  0.0285            *
## 8  H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0249  -0.0511   0.1009  0.5208             
## 9  F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0052  -0.0479   0.0374  0.8098
# R-squared comparison
print("\nR-SQUARED COMPARISON:")
## [1] "\nR-SQUARED COMPARISON:"
cat("Prosocial Behavior (6-factor): R² = ", round(inspect(prosocial_6factor.fit, "r2"), 3), "\n")
## Prosocial Behavior (6-factor): R² =  0.013
cat("Positive Reciprocity (6-factor): R² = ", round(inspect(posrec_6factor.fit, "r2"), 3), "\n") 
## Positive Reciprocity (6-factor): R² =  0.008
cat("Negative Reciprocity (6-factor): R² = ", round(inspect(negrec_6factor.fit, "r2"), 3), "\n")
## Negative Reciprocity (6-factor): R² =  0.014
print("\nPROSOCIAL MODEL (6-Factor) - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nPROSOCIAL MODEL (6-Factor) - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
print(prosocial_6factor_results)
##         Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1  D_self_end_CWC  -0.0042  -0.0229   0.0146  0.6650             
## 2 D_other_end_CWC   0.0822   0.0648   0.0997  0.0000          ***
## 3  H_self_end_CWC  -0.0254  -0.0457  -0.0050  0.0147            *
## 4 H_other_end_CWC  -0.0105  -0.0306   0.0096  0.3077             
## 5  F_self_end_CWC  -0.0258  -0.0459  -0.0056  0.0122            *
## 6 F_other_end_CWC   0.0437   0.0269   0.0605  0.0000          ***
## 7  D_norm_agg_CGM   0.0757   0.0350   0.1165  0.0003          ***
## 8  H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0832   0.0362   0.1302  0.0005          ***
## 9  F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0646  -0.1217  -0.0075  0.0265            *
print("\nPOSITIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL (6-Factor) - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nPOSITIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL (6-Factor) - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
print(posrec_6factor_results)
##         Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1  D_self_end_CWC   0.0091  -0.0071   0.0253  0.2721             
## 2 D_other_end_CWC   0.0657   0.0483   0.0830  0.0000          ***
## 3  H_self_end_CWC  -0.0348  -0.0538  -0.0159  0.0003          ***
## 4 H_other_end_CWC  -0.0041  -0.0251   0.0168  0.6986             
## 5  F_self_end_CWC   0.0131  -0.0102   0.0365  0.2706             
## 6 F_other_end_CWC   0.0286   0.0099   0.0472  0.0027           **
## 7  D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0092  -0.0490   0.0306  0.6512             
## 8  H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0032  -0.0405   0.0469  0.8852             
## 9  F_norm_agg_CGM   0.0266  -0.0181   0.0712  0.2439
print("\nNEGATIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL (6-Factor) - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:")
## [1] "\nNEGATIVE RECIPROCITY MODEL (6-Factor) - Standardized Coefficients with 95% CI:"
print(negrec_6factor_results)
##         Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1  D_self_end_CWC   0.0102  -0.0081   0.0285  0.2747             
## 2 D_other_end_CWC  -0.0256  -0.0458  -0.0054  0.0131            *
## 3  H_self_end_CWC   0.0795   0.0551   0.1040  0.0000          ***
## 4 H_other_end_CWC   0.0180  -0.0014   0.0374  0.0688             
## 5  F_self_end_CWC   0.0206  -0.0029   0.0441  0.0851             
## 6 F_other_end_CWC  -0.0522  -0.0775  -0.0268  0.0001          ***
## 7  D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0626  -0.1186  -0.0066  0.0285            *
## 8  H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0249  -0.0511   0.1009  0.5208             
## 9  F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0052  -0.0479   0.0374  0.8098
# ============================================================================
# SELF-REPORTED RECIPROCITY ANALYSIS - MULTILEVEL PATH MODELS
# Using dt4 dataset with Positive_Reciprocity and Negative_Reciprocity outcomes
# ============================================================================

# ============================================================================
# PART A: MODELS WITH NORM-BASED COUNTRY-LEVEL PREDICTORS
# ============================================================================


# STEP 1A: Prepare data with norm-based country predictors (3-factor individual)
print("Preparing data for 3-factor individual + norm-based country models...")
## [1] "Preparing data for 3-factor individual + norm-based country models..."
DF_selfreport_norms_3factor <- dt4 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Positive_Reciprocity) & !is.na(Negative_Reciprocity) &
           !is.na(D_end) & !is.na(H_end) & !is.na(F_end) &
           !is.na(D_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(H_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(F_norm_mean_country)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Individual-level variables: 3-factor model with group-mean centering (CWC)
    D_end_CWC = center(D_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_end_CWC = center(H_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_end_CWC = center(F_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    
    # Country-level variables: norm-based grand-mean centering (CGM)
    D_norm_agg_CGM = center(D_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    H_norm_agg_CGM = center(H_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    F_norm_agg_CGM = center(F_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM")
  )

print(paste("3-factor norm-based model sample size:", nrow(DF_selfreport_norms_3factor)))
## [1] "3-factor norm-based model sample size: 23557"
# STEP 2A: Prepare data with norm-based country predictors (6-factor individual)
print("Preparing data for 6-factor individual + norm-based country models...")
## [1] "Preparing data for 6-factor individual + norm-based country models..."
DF_selfreport_norms_6factor <- dt4 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Positive_Reciprocity) & !is.na(Negative_Reciprocity) &
           !is.na(D_self_end) & !is.na(D_other_end) & 
           !is.na(H_self_end) & !is.na(H_other_end) & 
           !is.na(F_self_end) & !is.na(F_other_end) &
           !is.na(D_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(H_norm_mean_country) & !is.na(F_norm_mean_country)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Individual-level variables: 6-factor model with group-mean centering (CWC)
    D_self_end_CWC = center(D_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    D_other_end_CWC = center(D_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_self_end_CWC = center(H_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_other_end_CWC = center(H_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_self_end_CWC = center(F_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_other_end_CWC = center(F_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    
    # Country-level variables: norm-based grand-mean centering (CGM)
    D_norm_agg_CGM = center(D_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    H_norm_agg_CGM = center(H_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    F_norm_agg_CGM = center(F_norm_mean_country, type = "CGM")
  )

print(paste("6-factor norm-based model sample size:", nrow(DF_selfreport_norms_6factor)))
## [1] "6-factor norm-based model sample size: 23557"
# ============================================================================
# PART B: MODELS WITH ENDORSEMENT-BASED COUNTRY-LEVEL PREDICTORS
# ============================================================================

# STEP 1B: Prepare data with endorsement-based country predictors (3-factor individual)
print("Preparing data for 3-factor individual + endorsement-based country models...")
## [1] "Preparing data for 3-factor individual + endorsement-based country models..."
DF_selfreport_endorse_3factor <- dt4 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Positive_Reciprocity) & !is.na(Negative_Reciprocity) &
           !is.na(D_end) & !is.na(H_end) & !is.na(F_end) &
           !is.na(D_end_mean_country) & !is.na(H_end_mean_country) & !is.na(F_end_mean_country)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Individual-level variables: 3-factor model with group-mean centering (CWC)
    D_end_CWC = center(D_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_end_CWC = center(H_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_end_CWC = center(F_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    
    # Country-level variables: endorsement-based grand-mean centering (CGM)
    D_end_agg_CGM = center(D_end_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    H_end_agg_CGM = center(H_end_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    F_end_agg_CGM = center(F_end_mean_country, type = "CGM")
  )

print(paste("3-factor endorsement-based model sample size:", nrow(DF_selfreport_endorse_3factor)))
## [1] "3-factor endorsement-based model sample size: 23557"
# STEP 2B: Prepare data with endorsement-based country predictors (6-factor individual)
print("Preparing data for 6-factor individual + endorsement-based country models...")
## [1] "Preparing data for 6-factor individual + endorsement-based country models..."
DF_selfreport_endorse_6factor <- dt4 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Positive_Reciprocity) & !is.na(Negative_Reciprocity) &
           !is.na(D_self_end) & !is.na(D_other_end) & 
           !is.na(H_self_end) & !is.na(H_other_end) & 
           !is.na(F_self_end) & !is.na(F_other_end) &
           !is.na(D_end_mean_country) & !is.na(H_end_mean_country) & !is.na(F_end_mean_country)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Individual-level variables: 6-factor model with group-mean centering (CWC)
    D_self_end_CWC = center(D_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    D_other_end_CWC = center(D_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_self_end_CWC = center(H_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    H_other_end_CWC = center(H_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_self_end_CWC = center(F_self_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    F_other_end_CWC = center(F_other_end, type = "CWC", cluster = country_res_full),
    
    # Country-level variables: endorsement-based grand-mean centering (CGM)
    D_end_agg_CGM = center(D_end_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    H_end_agg_CGM = center(H_end_mean_country, type = "CGM"),
    F_end_agg_CGM = center(F_end_mean_country, type = "CGM")
  )

print(paste("6-factor endorsement-based model sample size:", nrow(DF_selfreport_endorse_6factor)))
## [1] "6-factor endorsement-based model sample size: 23557"
# ============================================================================
# MODEL 1: POSITIVE RECIPROCITY - 3-FACTOR INDIVIDUAL + NORM-BASED COUNTRY
# ============================================================================

posrec_3factor_norms.model <- '
# Level 1: Individual endorsement predicting positive reciprocity
Positive_Reciprocity ~ D_end_CWC + H_end_CWC + F_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting positive reciprocity
Positive_Reciprocity ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

posrec_3factor_norms.fit <- sem(
  model = posrec_3factor_norms.model,
  data = DF_selfreport_norms_3factor,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("POSITIVE RECIPROCITY - 3-Factor Individual + Norm-Based Country:")
## [1] "POSITIVE RECIPROCITY - 3-Factor Individual + Norm-Based Country:"
print(summary(posrec_3factor_norms.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                         8
## 
##   Number of observations                         23557
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled
##   Test Statistic                                 0.000       0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0           0
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                              1550.021     168.086
##   Degrees of freedom                                 6           6
##   P-value                                        0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  9.222
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    1.000       1.000
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       1.000       1.000
##                                                                   
##   Robust Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                            NA
##   Robust Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                               NA
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)             -24770.060  -24770.060
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)     -24770.060  -24770.060
##                                                                   
##   Akaike (AIC)                               49556.120   49556.120
##   Bayesian (BIC)                             49620.657   49620.657
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)      49595.233   49595.233
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.000          NA
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.000          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                       NA          NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                       NA          NA
##                                                                   
##   Robust RMSEA                                               0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower                     0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper                     0.000
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA <= 0.050                            NA
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA >= 0.080                            NA
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.000       0.000
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                       Robust.cluster
##   Information                                 Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Regressions:
##                          Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   Positive_Reciprocity ~                                                      
##     D_end_CWC               0.265    0.025   10.442    0.000    0.265    0.218
##     H_end_CWC               0.034    0.011    3.067    0.002    0.034    0.033
##     F_end_CWC               0.040    0.011    3.740    0.000    0.040    0.039
##     D_norm_agg_CGM          0.087    0.055    1.596    0.111    0.087    0.043
##     H_norm_agg_CGM          0.113    0.125    0.902    0.367    0.113    0.033
##     F_norm_agg_CGM         -0.256    0.117   -2.186    0.029   -0.256   -0.094
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .Positv_Rcprcty    5.515    0.014  390.382    0.000    5.515    7.706
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .Positv_Rcprcty    0.480    0.023   20.952    0.000    0.480    0.936
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     Positv_Rcprcty    0.064
# Extract standardized results
posrec_3factor_norms_std <- standardizedSolution(posrec_3factor_norms.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
posrec_3factor_norms_reg <- posrec_3factor_norms_std[posrec_3factor_norms_std$op == "~",]
posrec_3factor_norms_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = posrec_3factor_norms_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(posrec_3factor_norms_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(posrec_3factor_norms_reg$ci.lower, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(posrec_3factor_norms_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(posrec_3factor_norms_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(posrec_3factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(posrec_3factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(posrec_3factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(posrec_3factor_norms_results)
##        Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1      D_end_CWC   0.2176   0.1784   0.2568  0.0000          ***
## 2      H_end_CWC   0.0335   0.0117   0.0552  0.0025           **
## 3      F_end_CWC   0.0393   0.0194   0.0591  0.0001          ***
## 4 D_norm_agg_CGM   0.0427  -0.0101   0.0955  0.1130             
## 5 H_norm_agg_CGM   0.0329  -0.0382   0.1039  0.3647             
## 6 F_norm_agg_CGM  -0.0938  -0.1778  -0.0099  0.0285            *
# ============================================================================
# MODEL 2: NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY - 3-FACTOR INDIVIDUAL + NORM-BASED COUNTRY
# ============================================================================

negrec_3factor_norms.model <- '
# Level 1: Individual endorsement predicting negative reciprocity
Negative_Reciprocity ~ D_end_CWC + H_end_CWC + F_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting negative reciprocity
Negative_Reciprocity ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

negrec_3factor_norms.fit <- sem(
  model = negrec_3factor_norms.model,
  data = DF_selfreport_norms_3factor,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY - 3-Factor Individual + Norm-Based Country:")
## [1] "NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY - 3-Factor Individual + Norm-Based Country:"
print(summary(negrec_3factor_norms.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                         8
## 
##   Number of observations                         23557
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled

## 
## Regressions:
##                          Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   Negative_Reciprocity ~                                                      
##     D_end_CWC              -0.481    0.020  -23.654    0.000   -0.481   -0.199
##     H_end_CWC               0.534    0.025   21.301    0.000    0.534    0.263
##     F_end_CWC              -0.141    0.023   -6.225    0.000   -0.141   -0.071
##     D_norm_agg_CGM         -0.517    0.106   -4.862    0.000   -0.517   -0.128
##     H_norm_agg_CGM         -0.906    0.238   -3.810    0.000   -0.906   -0.133
##     F_norm_agg_CGM          1.200    0.159    7.530    0.000    1.200    0.222
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .Negatv_Rcprcty    2.917    0.036   80.704    0.000    2.917    2.056
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .Negatv_Rcprcty    1.815    0.057   31.639    0.000    1.815    0.902
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     Negatv_Rcprcty    0.098
# Extract standardized results
negrec_3factor_norms_std <- standardizedSolution(negrec_3factor_norms.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
negrec_3factor_norms_reg <- negrec_3factor_norms_std[negrec_3factor_norms_std$op == "~",]
negrec_3factor_norms_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = negrec_3factor_norms_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(negrec_3factor_norms_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(negrec_3factor_norms_reg$ci.lower, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(negrec_3factor_norms_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(negrec_3factor_norms_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(negrec_3factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(negrec_3factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(negrec_3factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(negrec_3factor_norms_results)
##        Parameter Std_Beta CI_Lower CI_Upper p_value Significance
## 1      D_end_CWC  -0.1989  -0.2181  -0.1798   0e+00          ***
## 2      H_end_CWC   0.2634   0.2395   0.2873   0e+00          ***
## 3      F_end_CWC  -0.0708  -0.0928  -0.0487   0e+00          ***
## 4 D_norm_agg_CGM  -0.1277  -0.1780  -0.0774   0e+00          ***
## 5 H_norm_agg_CGM  -0.1333  -0.2015  -0.0652   1e-04          ***
## 6 F_norm_agg_CGM   0.2222   0.1655   0.2790   0e+00          ***
# ============================================================================
# MODEL 3: POSITIVE RECIPROCITY - 6-FACTOR INDIVIDUAL + NORM-BASED COUNTRY
# ============================================================================

posrec_6factor_norms.model <- '
# Level 1: 6-factor individual endorsement predicting positive reciprocity
Positive_Reciprocity ~ D_self_end_CWC + D_other_end_CWC + 
                      H_self_end_CWC + H_other_end_CWC + 
                      F_self_end_CWC + F_other_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting positive reciprocity
Positive_Reciprocity ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

posrec_6factor_norms.fit <- sem(
  model = posrec_6factor_norms.model,
  data = DF_selfreport_norms_6factor,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("POSITIVE RECIPROCITY - 6-Factor Individual + Norm-Based Country:")
## [1] "POSITIVE RECIPROCITY - 6-Factor Individual + Norm-Based Country:"
print(summary(posrec_6factor_norms.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 1 iteration
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB

## 
##   Number of observations                         23557
##   Number of clusters [country_res_full]             63

## Regressions:
##                          Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   Positive_Reciprocity ~                                                      
##     D_self_end_CWC          0.112    0.014    8.112    0.000    0.112    0.103
##     D_other_nd_CWC          0.141    0.015    9.339    0.000    0.141    0.143
##     H_self_end_CWC         -0.038    0.011   -3.379    0.001   -0.038   -0.042
##     H_other_nd_CWC          0.079    0.009    8.917    0.000    0.079    0.092
##     F_self_end_CWC          0.065    0.010    6.369    0.000    0.065    0.070
##     F_other_nd_CWC         -0.036    0.011   -3.347    0.001   -0.036   -0.041
##     D_norm_agg_CGM          0.087    0.055    1.596    0.111    0.087    0.043
##     H_norm_agg_CGM          0.113    0.125    0.902    0.367    0.113    0.033
##     F_norm_agg_CGM         -0.256    0.117   -2.186    0.029   -0.256   -0.094
## 
## Intercepts:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .Positv_Rcprcty    5.515    0.014  390.382    0.000    5.515    7.706
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .Positv_Rcprcty    0.476    0.022   21.173    0.000    0.476    0.929
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     Positv_Rcprcty    0.071
# Extract standardized results
posrec_6factor_norms_std <- standardizedSolution(posrec_6factor_norms.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
posrec_6factor_norms_reg <- posrec_6factor_norms_std[posrec_6factor_norms_std$op == "~",]
posrec_6factor_norms_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = posrec_6factor_norms_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(posrec_6factor_norms_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(posrec_6factor_norms_reg$ci.lower, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(posrec_6factor_norms_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(posrec_6factor_norms_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(posrec_6factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(posrec_6factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(posrec_6factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(posrec_6factor_norms_results)

## 5  F_self_end_CWC   0.0699   0.0500   0.0898  0.0000          ***
## 6 F_other_end_CWC  -0.0412  -0.0651  -0.0173  0.0007          ***

# ============================================================================
# MODEL 4: NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY - 6-FACTOR INDIVIDUAL + NORM-BASED COUNTRY
# ============================================================================

negrec_6factor_norms.model <- '
# Level 1: 6-factor individual endorsement predicting negative reciprocity
Negative_Reciprocity ~ D_self_end_CWC + D_other_end_CWC + 
                      H_self_end_CWC + H_other_end_CWC + 
                      F_self_end_CWC + F_other_end_CWC

# Level 2: Country-level norms predicting negative reciprocity
Negative_Reciprocity ~ D_norm_agg_CGM + H_norm_agg_CGM + F_norm_agg_CGM
'

negrec_6factor_norms.fit <- sem(
  model = negrec_6factor_norms.model,
  data = DF_selfreport_norms_6factor,
  cluster = "country_res_full"
)

print("NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY - 6-Factor Individual + Norm-Based Country:")
## [1] "NEGATIVE RECIPROCITY - 6-Factor Individual + Norm-Based Country:"
print(summary(negrec_6factor_norms.fit, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE))

# Extract standardized results
negrec_6factor_norms_std <- standardizedSolution(negrec_6factor_norms.fit, ci = TRUE, level = 0.95)
negrec_6factor_norms_reg <- negrec_6factor_norms_std[negrec_6factor_norms_std$op == "~",]
negrec_6factor_norms_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = negrec_6factor_norms_reg$rhs,
  Std_Beta = round(negrec_6factor_norms_reg$est.std, 4),
  CI_Lower = round(negrec_6factor_norms_reg$ci.lower, 4),
  CI_Upper = round(negrec_6factor_norms_reg$ci.upper, 4),
  p_value = round(negrec_6factor_norms_reg$pvalue, 4),
  Significance = ifelse(negrec_6factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(negrec_6factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(negrec_6factor_norms_reg$pvalue < 0.05, "*", "")))
)
print(negrec_6factor_norms_results)
